Perform one of the following depending on what your connectivity issue is:To troubleshoot the connectivity to the iSCSI storage using the software initiatorCheck whether a ping to the storage array fails from the host. For more information, see Testing VMkernel network connectivity with the vmkping command (1003728).Check that the initiator is registered on the array. Contact your storage vendor for instructions on this procedure.Check that the following physical hardware is correctly functioning:Ethernet switchEthernet cables between the switch and the hostEthernet cables between the switch and the storage arrayTo troubleshoot the connectivity to the fiber attached storage, check the following:The fiber switch zoning configuration permits the host to see the storage array. Consult your switch vendor if you require assistance.The fiber switch propagates RSCN messages to the hosts. For more information on configuring the fiber switch. Configuring fibre switch so that ESX Server doesn't require a reboot after a zone set change (1002301).Check the physical hardware for the following:The storage processors on the array.The fiber switch and the Gigabit Interface Converter (GBIC) units in the switch.The fiber cables between the fiber switch and the array.The array itself.Important: A rescan is required after any change is made to see if the targets are detectedRefer to this VMware KB article for more information
knowledgeExternal: In case of NFS datastores being used please refer to this VMware KB article Troubleshooting connectivity issues to an NFS datastore for more information

name: Host CPU usage
knowledgeSummary: This host has exceeded the threshold for CPU usage
knowledgeCause: This is used to measure the total processor utilization of the hypervisor for the entire system. Host logical processor correlates directly to the number of processors or cores that are installed on the physical computer. For example, 2 quad core processors installed on the physical computer would correlate to 8 logical processors. One of the main reasons for high CPU Usage is a CPU demand for VMs located on Hyper-V host
knowledgeResolution: View the host CPU usage tab for historical information. Check the allocation of CPU resources for VMs on Hyper-V server. Check historical CPU usage, number of VMs running, and other performance factors. Check for alerts for VMs for high CPU usage

name: VM CPU usage
knowledgeSummary: This VM has exceeded the threshold for CPU usage
knowledgeCause: This counter shows the percentage of processor time spent in guest and hypervisor code for a specific VM. Virtual processors are what the virtual machines actually use, and all execution in the root and child partitions occurs in virtual processors. Most likely high demand for CPU resources is caused by application workloads inside the VM
knowledgeResolution: Check the VM CPU usage tab. Also check for high demand for CPU resources from application workloads inside the VM

name: Host network outbound errors number
knowledgeSummary: This host has exceeded the threshold for the outbound packets that couldn't be transmitted because of errors
knowledgeCause: If the error counts grow rapidly you might have too much load on the system or some problem in end- to-end connectivity (including hardware). This error is most likely caused by a faulty or damaged network interface card (NIC)
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this error, you must determine the cause of the outgoing packet errors, and then correct the problem. If the outgoing packet errors are caused by a faulty NIC, replace the card immediately

name: Host network average output queue length
knowledgeSummary: This host has exceeded the threshold for the length of the queue in packets. This counter should be 0 at all times
knowledgeCause: The output queue length measures the number of threads waiting on the network adapter. If there are more than 2 threads waiting on the network adapter, then the network may be a bottleneck. Common causes of this are poor network latency and/or high collision rates on the network
knowledgeResolution: If an output queue length of 2 or more is measured, consider adding one or more physical network adapters to the physical computer that hosts the virtual machines and bind the network adapters used by the guest operating systems to these physical network adapters

name: Host average disk queue length
knowledgeSummary: Average disk queue length on the host may report on too many I/O requests. This means that not all requests are queued. Some requests are completed and are on their way back to where the performance data is being collected
knowledgeCause: As a general rule for hard disks, an Avg Disk Queue Length greater than 2 (per hard disk) for extended periods of time is considered undesirable. If you have a RAID system with 8 disks, you do not want an Avg Disk Queue Length greater than 16. A queue length of 32 might indicate this disk is saturated and is the bottleneck in the system
knowledgeResolution: Faster hard disks with quicker access times (and therefore I/O) will allow greater flexibility with Avg Disk Queue Length numbers. Also if you have any concurrently running resource intensive tasks (backup jobs, antivirus checks etc.), consider using different time schedule in order to offload you host disks

name: VM disk errors
knowledgeSummary: This VM has logged one or more errors that have occurred on its virtual device
knowledgeCause: The error count should always be zero for the virtual storage device
knowledgeResolution: Check the status of the disk subsystem and connectivity. Check for possible disk hardware, firmware or driver issues. See historical graph of error counts

name: Current memory pressure
knowledgeSummary: This VM has exceeded the threshold for memory pressure
knowledgeCause: Dynamic Memory determines the amount of memory needed by a virtual machine by calculating memory pressure. To perform this calculation, Hyper-V looks at the total committed memory of the guest operating system running in the virtual machine and then calculates pressure as the ratio of how much memory the virtual machine wants to how much it has. The higher VM average pressure you have, the more VM starts paging memory to disk with disastrous effect on VM's performance
knowledgeResolution: Most likely high demand for Memory resources is caused by application workloads inside the VM, to troubleshoot memory pressure check dynamic memory allocation, buffer, and limits for this VM. If there is not enough physical memory available on the host to give virtual machine its requested amount of memory, consider relocating this VM to another host or verify memory usage for existing VMs on the same host

name: Host available memory
knowledgeSummary: This host is low on available memory
knowledgeCause: Available Memory (%) measures the amount of physical memory available to processes running on the computer, as a percentage of physical memory installed on the computer. This is memory host will use for new tasks, e.g. to start new VMs or to satisfy new memory demands from running VMs
knowledgeResolution: View the host memory usage tab for historical information. Check the allocation of memory resources for VMs on Hyper-V server. Check historical memory usage, number of VMs running, and other performance factors. Check for alerts for VMs with high memory pressure

name: Host average memory pressure
knowledgeSummary: This host has exceeded the threshold for memory pressure
knowledgeCause: This metric is expressed as a percentage, which can go above 100%. It shows potential memory over-commitment issues on the host
knowledgeResolution: When this value exceeds 90% memory is generally referred to as "over-commitment". It may not indicate a performance problem as such, however, you should be aware of the potential performance impact. If this metric exceeds the threshold, then you should check historical memory pressure usage, number of VMs running, and other performance factors. Check for alerts for VMs with high memory pressure

name: Failed to create cluster resource name in domain
knowledgeSummary: Cluster network name resource failed to create its associated computer object in domain
knowledgeCause: Cluster identity might not be able to create computer objects in the domain
knowledgeResolution: Please work with your domain administrator to ensure that:The cluster identity can create computer objects. By default all computer objects are created in the 'Computers' container; consult the domain administrator if this location has been changed.The quota for computer objects has not been reached.If there is an existing computer object, verify the Cluster Identity has 'Full Control' permission to that computer object using the Active Directory Users and Computers tool.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Active Directory Permissions for Cluster Accounts event (ID 1193) description for more information

name: Cluster resource cannot be brought online
knowledgeSummary: Cluster network name resource cannot be brought online. The computer object associated with the resource could not be updated in domain
knowledgeCause: The cluster identity may lack permissions required to update the object
knowledgeResolution: Please work with your domain administrator to ensure that the cluster identity can update computer objects in the domain
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Active Directory Permissions for Cluster Accounts event (ID 1207) description for more information

name: Failed to register VM configuration file
knowledgeSummary: The Hyper-V Virtual Machine Management service failed to register the configuration for the virtual machine
knowledgeCause: Clustered resources might not be in a healthy state
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that you have the appropriate permissions on the target machine, and check the configuration settings of the virtual machine. Then try the planned failover operation again

name: Failed to unregister VM configuration file
knowledgeSummary: The Hyper-V Virtual Machine Management service failed to unregister the configuration for the virtual machine
knowledgeCause: Changes to registration of the virtual machine resource are not allowed when the virtual machine is running
knowledgeResolution: Check virtual machine registration. Turn off the virtual machine, manually unregister the virtual machine and delete the corresponding configuration files

name: Failed to verify VM configuration file
knowledgeSummary: The Hyper-V Virtual Machine Management service failed to verify that the configuration is registered for the virtual machine
knowledgeCause: All the clustered resources might not be in a healthy state
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that you have the appropriate permissions on the target machine, and check the configuration settings of the virtual machine. Then try the planned failover operation again

name: VSS checkpoint failure
knowledgeSummary: Failed to create the backup of virtual machine
knowledgeCause: Usually this alarm is caused by VSS checkpoint failure
knowledgeResolution: Please check the event log in the guest to analyze why the VSS checkpoint failed and then retry the operation
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Virtual Machine Integration Services event (ID 10102) description for more information

name: Failed to revert to VSS snapshot
knowledgeSummary: Failed to revert to VSS snapshot on one or more virtual hard disks of the virtual machine
knowledgeCause: Integration services might not be running or guest Hyper-V drivers are not in a healthy state
knowledgeResolution: Retry the VSS Snapshot operation. Verify that integration services are all shown in a running state in the service control manager of the virtual machine. All the guest Hyper-V drivers are in a healthy state. Additionally, any operations that involve the integration services complete successfully
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Virtual Machine Integration Services event (ID 10104) description for more information

name: Failed to power on VM
knowledgeSummary: Failed to power on VM
knowledgeCause: Check corresponding event message for more information
knowledgeResolution: Check the environment the virtual machine is trying to start in:the virtual machine RAM should be less than the physical memory available the number of virtual processors should not exceed number of logical processors on the systemthe eventlog (for any prior failures that lead to this failure)Try starting the virtual machine again

name: Failed to save VM
knowledgeSummary: Failed to save state for a VM
knowledgeCause: There might not be sufficient disk space to hold VM save state file
knowledgeResolution: Check the environment that you are trying to save the virtual machine in. For example, ensure you have sufficient disk space to hold the save state file, and then try saving the state of the virtual machine again

name: Failed to restore VM
knowledgeSummary: Failed to restore a VM
knowledgeCause: Check corresponding event message for more details on the reasons for this failure
knowledgeResolution: Check the environment the virtual machine is trying to start in:the virtual machine RAM should be less than the physical memory availablethe number of virtual processors should not exceed number of logical processors on the systemthe eventlog (for any prior failures that lead to this failure)Try starting the virtual machine again

name: Failed to open VM attachment
knowledgeSummary: Cannot open VM attachment
knowledgeCause: There may be multiple issues. You should start with the error message. For example, if the error message states the image "may have been removed from the system" begin by checking the location of the file. If the file is present, check permissions issues
knowledgeResolution: Check and ensure a valid image exists, has the right permissions, and is attached. You may get this failure if the image, connection, and/or permissions are mismatched. For example, an image file can be a virtual hard disk (.vhd file), or a DVD disc (.iso file). If a text file with a .txt file name extension were renamed with a .vhd file extension, you would get an error that the image is not valid. If you attempted to attach a .vhd virtual hard disk to a SCSI controller and that controller is not supported by the operating system, you would also get an error. You might also get this error if the image is valid, but in a different location than specified, or in the location specified, but not accessible by the user credential

name: Machine remoting system failure
knowledgeSummary: Failure in machine remoting system has been detected
knowledgeCause: Please review the corresponding event message for more information
knowledgeResolution: Please close the current vmconnect session and start a new session

name: Invalid static MAC address
knowledgeSummary: By default, new virtual machines in Hyper-V are created with NICs that are assigned dynamic MAC addresses
knowledgeCause: If virtual machines with identical static MAC addresses are run at the same time, virtual machine networking may not work properly
knowledgeResolution: For virtual machines that share a virtualization server (also called a host), static MAC address conflicts are rarely an issue. However, if multiple servers with the Hyper-V role enabled are used, you must ensure that the address ranges are unique and managed across the servers
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's NIC Configuration event (ID 12560) description for more information

name: Static MAC address conflict
knowledgeSummary: By default, new virtual machines in Hyper-V are created with NICs that are assigned dynamic MAC addresses
knowledgeCause: If virtual machines with identical static MAC addresses are run at the same time, virtual machine networking may not work properly
knowledgeResolution: For virtual machines that share a virtualization server (also called a host), static MAC address conflicts are rarely an issue. However, if multiple servers with the Hyper-V role enabled are used, you must ensure that the address ranges are unique and managed across the servers
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's NIC Configuration event (ID 12562) description for more information

name: VM invalid switch port reference
knowledgeSummary: The virtual machine cannot be started
knowledgeCause: VM virtual network adapter is configured to use a network that is unavailable
knowledgeResolution: To fix this problem, change the virtual machine NIC settings to point to a different/valid switch port, and then restart the virtual machine

name: No disk space to run this VM
knowledgeSummary: VM has been paused because it has run out of disk space
knowledgeCause: There is no free disk space to run this VM
knowledgeResolution: Check disk space. Turn Off the virtual machine, and then move the virtual hard disk associated with the virtual machine to a volume with additional disk space. Reconfigure the virtual machine to point to the new location of the virtual hard disk and then restart the virtual machine

name: Failed to delete VM directory
knowledgeSummary: Cannot delete VM directory
knowledgeCause: File or directory might be missing or the account used to delete it doesn't have sufficient permissions
knowledgeResolution: Please ensure that the file/directory exists and has the right permissions before trying to delete it again

name: Failed to merge virtual disk
knowledgeSummary: Cannot merge disk file on deletion. As a result, this disk might be in inconsistent state
knowledgeCause: Virtual disk might be locked by some process which prevents it from merging successfully
knowledgeResolution: The parent virtual hard disks associated with this virtual machine may be in an inconsistent state because of a failed merge operation. Please discard this image and create a new one for future use

name: Failed to load VM configuration
knowledgeSummary: Cannot load a virtual machine configuration
knowledgeCause: Virtual machine configuration file is set with incorrect parameters or VMMS service cannot access it
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that the virtual machine configuration file is set with the right parameters. Additionally, ensure that the configuration file has the right permissions set so that the VMMS service can access it. Then, retry the operation that failed

name: VM configuration file is corrupt
knowledgeSummary: Cannot load the virtual machine because the configuration is corrupt
knowledgeCause: If you receive this message during VM import procedure, then most likely this VM was not exported properly
knowledgeResolution: Delete the virtual machine configuration file (.xml file) and recreate the virtual machine

name: VM configuration is not accessible
knowledgeSummary: The configuration of virtual machine is no longer accessible
knowledgeCause: Virtual machine configuration files might not be accessible due insufficient permissions or incorrect location path
knowledgeResolution: Check the configuration associated with this snapshot and ensure that it has the right file path permissions as well as the right parameters. Then, retry the operation

name: Checkpoint configuration is not accessible
knowledgeSummary: The configuration of checkpoint is no longer accessible
knowledgeCause: Virtual machine checkpoint files might not be accessible due insufficient permissions or incorrect location path
knowledgeResolution: Check the configuration associated with this snapshot and ensure that it has the right file path permissions as well as the right parameters. Then, retry the operation

name: Failed to migrate virtual machine
knowledgeSummary: Live migration for this VM did not succeed
knowledgeCause: There might be multiple reasons for this issue:There is connectivity issue between two nodes. Other node has Virtual Network setup incorrectly. The configuration validation doesn't pass and have some SAN/iSCSI issues.
knowledgeResolution: Verify that there is no connectivity issue between the host nodes, destination node has Virtual Network setup correctly and make sure that configuration validation passes and doesn't have SAN/iSCSI issues

name: VM initialization error
knowledgeSummary: VM initialization has failed
knowledgeCause: There might be multiple reasons for this failure. See resolution section for more information
knowledgeResolution: Check the environment in which the start virtual machine operation was being attempted, including:access and permissions to the configuration file, memory file and all image files prior error messages in the event logavailable RAM on the system configuration settings disk space for the memory file and any expanding virtual hard disksOnce the necessary issues have been rectified, try to start the virtual machine again. If the problem continues, restart the VMMS Service

name: Failed to initialize VM memory
knowledgeSummary: Failed to initialize VM memory
knowledgeCause: There might be multiple reasons for this error:not available RAM on the systeminvalid configuration settingsnot enough disk space for the memory file and any expanding virtual hard disks
knowledgeResolution: Retry virtual machine start. If the problem continues, restart the VMMS Service and try again

name: Failed to create VM saved state file
knowledgeSummary: Failed to create or access VM saved state file
knowledgeCause: There might be multiple reasons for this failure. See resolution section for more information
knowledgeResolution: Investigate the environment in which the virtual machine operation was attempted, including: access and permissions to the configuration file, memory file and all image files prior error messages in the event logavailable RAM on the systemconfiguration settingsdisk space for the memory file and any expanding virtual hard disksaccess and permissions to the saved state file

name: Not enough memory to start a VM
knowledgeSummary: Hyper-V was unable to allocate RAM resources to start this VM
knowledgeCause: There is not enough free RAM resources to start VMs on this host
knowledgeResolution: To resolve that, please reconfigure your VM to use less RAM or move this VM to a different host with sufficient RAM resources

name: Failed to create memory contents file
knowledgeSummary: Failed to create memory contents file
knowledgeCause: Virtual machine is configured with too much RAM
knowledgeResolution: Reduce the required RAM for the virtual machine and try to start the virtual machine again

name: Incompatible version of integration services
knowledgeSummary: The version of a component of integration services is incompatible with another version
knowledgeCause: Hyper-V node connected to virtual machine, but the version does not match the version expected by Hyper-V
knowledgeResolution: To fix this problem, remove the existing version from the operating system on the virtual machine. Ensure that you have installed the latest version of Hyper-V. Verify that integration services are all shown in a running state in the service control manager of the virtual machine. All the guest Hyper-V drivers are in a healthy state. Additionally, any operations that involve the integration services complete successfully
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Virtual Machine Integration Services documentation for more information

name: VM configuration module error
knowledgeSummary: The VM configuration is no longer accessible
knowledgeCause: Virtual machine might not have right permissions on vsconfig.dll file or VMMS service is not started
knowledgeResolution: Check that virtual machine configuration module (also called the "store") is contained in the vsconfig.dll file and the path to the vsconfig.dll file is valid and has the right permissions. By default the vsconfig.dll file is installed at \\windows\system32. Verify that the VMMS service is in the running state in the Service Control Manager and that the failed operation now succeeds
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's VMMS event (ID 4096) description for more information

name: Background disk merge interruption
knowledgeSummary: The snapshot merge operation was interrupted
knowledgeCause: This is usually because .avhd file is in use
knowledgeResolution: To merge the .avhd file to the parent, turn off the associated virtual machine
knowledgeExternal: A snapshot in Hyper-V is a file-based snapshot of the state, disk data, and configuration of a virtual machine at a specific point in time

name: Background disk merge failed
knowledgeSummary: The parent virtual hard disks associated with this virtual machine may be in an inconsistent state
knowledgeCause: Background disk merge failed to complete
knowledgeResolution: Please discard this image and create a new one for future use

name: Volume snapshot preparation error
knowledgeSummary: A backup application initiated a VSS snapshot on Cluster Shared Volume without properly preparing the volume for snapshot. This snapshot may be invalid and the backup may not be usable for restore operations
knowledgeCause: Because of the design of Cluster Shared Volumes, backup applications for these volumes must meet specific requirements for compatibility. The Cluster Shared Volumes feature allows volumes in cluster storage to be accessed by all nodes of a failover cluster. This makes it possible for virtual machines to use volumes on the same LUN (disk) while still being able to fail over (or move from node to node) independently of one another
knowledgeResolution: Please contact your backup application vendor to verify compatibility with Cluster Shared Volumes
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Backup event (ID 1584) description for more information

name: Cluster Shared Volume is not available
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume is no longer available on this node. All I/O will temporarily be queued until a path to the volume is reestablished
knowledgeCause: There has been an interruption to communication between a cluster node and a volume in Cluster Shared Volumes. This interruption may be short enough that it is not noticeable, or long enough that it interferes with services and applications using the volume
knowledgeResolution: If the interruption persists, review other events in the System or Application event logs for information about communication between the node and the volume
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Functionality event (ID 5120) description for more information

name: Cluster Shared Volume is not directly accessible
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume is no longer directly accessible from this cluster node. I/O access will be redirected to the storage device over the network through the node that owns the volume. This may result in degraded performance
knowledgeCause: Not all hosts might have network connection to a Cluster Shared Volume
knowledgeResolution: If redirected access is turned on for this volume, please turn it off. If redirected access is turned off, please troubleshoot this node's connectivity to the storage device and I/O will resume to a healthy state once connectivity to the storage device is reestablished
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Functionality event (ID 5121) description for more information

name: Active filter drivers detected
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume has identified one or more active filter drivers on this device stack that could interfere with CSV operations. I/O access will be redirected to the storage device over the network through another Cluster node. This may result in degraded performance
knowledgeCause: One or more active filter drivers on device stack have been detected
knowledgeResolution: Please contact the filter driver vendor to verify interoperability with Cluster Shared Volumes
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Functionality event (ID 5125) description for more information

name: Redirected access was turned on
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume redirected access was turned on. Access to the storage device will be redirected over the network from all cluster nodes that are accessing this volume. This may result in degraded performance
knowledgeCause: Check redirected access status for a disk
knowledgeResolution: Please turn off redirected access for this volume
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Functionality event (ID 5136) description for more information

name: Cluster Shared Volume is no longer accessible
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume is no longer accessible from this cluster node because of error
knowledgeCause: Network connection between host node and storage device might be broken
knowledgeResolution: Please troubleshoot this node's connectivity to the storage device and network connectivity
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Shared Volume Functionality event (ID 5142) description for more information

name: Cluster host node network connectivity error
knowledgeSummary: This cluster node has no network connectivity. It cannot participate in the cluster until connectivity is restored
knowledgeCause: There is no network connectivity for this cluster node
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapter. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Network Connectivity and Configuration event (ID 1554) description for more information

name: Host node failed to join cluster
knowledgeSummary: Host node failed to join the cluster because it could not send and receive failure detection network messages with other cluster nodes
knowledgeCause: Host failed to send and receive failure detection network messages to other cluster nodes
knowledgeResolution: Please run the Validate a Configuration wizard to ensure network settings. Also verify the Windows Firewall 'Failover Clusters' rules
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Network Connectivity and Configuration event (ID 1572) description for more information

name: Host node was removed from cluster
knowledgeSummary: Cluster node was removed from the active failover cluster membership. If the Cluster service fails to start on a failover cluster node, the node cannot function as part of the cluster
knowledgeCause: The Cluster service on this node may have stopped. This could also be due to the node having lost communication with other active nodes in the failover cluster
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapters on this node. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1135) description for more information

name: Missing latest cluster configuration data
knowledgeSummary: The cluster service has determined that this node does not have the latest copy of cluster configuration data. Therefore, the cluster service has prevented itself from starting on this node
knowledgeCause: Latest copy of cluster configuration data is missing for one of the nodes
knowledgeResolution: Try starting the cluster service on all nodes in the cluster. If the cluster service can be started on other nodes with the latest copy of the cluster configuration data, this node will be able to subsequently join the started cluster successfully
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1561) description for more information

name: Host node was evicted from cluster
knowledgeSummary: Cluster host node has been evicted from the failover cluster
knowledgeCause: Cluster service might not be responding on the host
knowledgeResolution: Review the event log for other events associated with this issue. You might be able to correct this issue by restarting the Cluster service. For more information, see "Restarting the Cluster service on a node."
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Node Membership in Cluster event (ID 1011) description for more information

name: Host cluster membership
knowledgeSummary: The Cluster service cannot identify host node as a member of failover cluster
knowledgeCause: Host name computer name change is the most common reason for this error
knowledgeResolution: If the computer name for this node was recently changed, consider reverting to the previous name. Alternatively, add the node to the failover cluster and if necessary, reinstall clustered applications
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Node Membership in Cluster event (ID 1093) description for more information

name: Host failed to form a cluster
knowledgeSummary: Host node failed to form a failover cluster
knowledgeCause: This was due to one or more nodes executing incompatible versions of the cluster service software
knowledgeResolution: If one of the hosts in the cluster has been recently upgraded, please re-verify that all nodes are executing compatible versions of the cluster service software
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Node Membership in Cluster event (ID 1546) description for more information

name: Cluster hosts update version mismatch
knowledgeSummary: Host node has established a communication session with another node and detected that it is running a different but compatible version of the cluster service software
knowledgeCause: Update version mismatch between hosts nodes in the cluster
knowledgeResolution: It is recommended that the same version of the cluster service software be installed on all nodes in the cluster
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Node Membership in Cluster event (ID 1548) description for more information

name: Cluster communication session failed
knowledgeSummary: Host mode failed to establish a communication session while joining the cluster
knowledgeCause: Authentication failure has occurred
knowledgeResolution: Please verify that the nodes are running compatible versions of the cluster service software
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Node Membership in Cluster event (ID 1570) description for more information

name: Unreachable cluster network interface
knowledgeSummary: Cluster network interface for cluster node is unreachable by at least one other cluster node attached to the network
knowledgeCause: The failover cluster was not able to determine the location of the failure
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapter. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Network Connectivity event (ID 1126) description for more information

name: Cluster network failure
knowledgeSummary: Cluster network interface for cluster node has failed
knowledgeCause: Network interface is no longer accessible
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapter. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Network Connectivity event (ID 1127) description for more information

name: Network communication failure
knowledgeSummary: Cluster node lost communication with another cluster node. Network communication was reestablished
knowledgeCause: This could be due to communication temporarily being blocked by a firewall or connection security policy update
knowledgeResolution: If the problem persists and network communication are not reestablished, the cluster service on one or more nodes will stop. If that happens, run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. Additionally, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapters on this node, and check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Network Connectivity event (ID 1592) description for more information

name: Cluster witness resource update failure
knowledgeSummary: Cluster service failed to update the cluster configuration data on the witness resource
knowledgeCause: Witness resource is offline and not accessible
knowledgeResolution: If you are not sure whether the cluster quorum configuration includes a witness disk or a witness file share, see "Viewing the quorum configuration of a failover cluster." Then confirm that the witness disk or witness file share is online. For more information about viewing the status of a witness disk, see "Viewing the status of a witness disk in a failover cluster that uses a quorum configuration of Node and Disk Majority."
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Witness Functionality event (ID 1557) description for more information

name: Cluster witness resource failure
knowledgeSummary: The cluster service detected a problem with the witness resource. The witness resource will be failed over to another node within the cluster in an attempt to reestablish access to cluster configuration data
knowledgeCause: Witness disk or witness file share is offline
knowledgeResolution: If you are not sure whether the cluster quorum configuration includes a witness disk or a witness file share, see "Viewing the quorum configuration of a failover cluster." Then confirm that the witness disk or witness file share is online. For more information about viewing the status of a witness disk, see "Viewing the status of a witness disk in a failover cluster that uses a quorum configuration of Node and Disk Majority."
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Witness Functionality event (ID 1558) description for more information

name: Host node failed to form a cluster
knowledgeSummary: Host node failed to form a cluster
knowledgeCause: This was because the witness was not accessible
knowledgeResolution: Please ensure that the witness resource is online and available
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Witness Functionality event (ID 1573) description for more information

name: Cluster resource failure
knowledgeSummary: Cluster resource in clustered service or application has failed
knowledgeCause: There might be problem with the application or service associated with the resource. Also this cluster resource might not be properly configured
knowledgeResolution: Check state of clustered resource and verify that the clustered service or application can come online, and observe whether additional events are logged regarding resources in the clustered service or application. Adjust properties for the resource in the cluster configuration, especially the value for Pending timeout for the resource
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Clustered Service or Application Availability event (ID 1069) description for more information

name: Failed to bring cluster resource online
knowledgeSummary: Cluster IP address resource cannot be brought online
knowledgeCause: Duplicate IP address is detected on the network
knowledgeResolution: Please ensure all IP addresses are unique
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's IP Address Resource Availability event (ID 1049) description for more information

name: Invalid IP address detected
knowledgeSummary: Cluster IP address resource cannot be brought online because the address value is invalid
knowledgeCause: Invalid IP address for this cluster resource has been detected
knowledgeResolution: Please check your IP address resource properties
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's IP Address Resource Availability event (ID 1047) description for more information

name: Invalid subnet mask detected
knowledgeSummary: Cluster IP address resource cannot be brought online because the subnet mask value is invalid
knowledgeCause: Cluster resource has invalid subnet mask value
knowledgeResolution: Please check your IP address resource properties
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's IP Address Resource Availability event (ID 1046) description for more information

name: Invalid IP address for cluster resource
knowledgeSummary: Cluster IP address resource failed to come online
knowledgeCause: VM has invalid IP address configured
knowledgeResolution: Ensure the network property matches a cluster network name or the address property matches one of the subnets on a cluster network
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Network Connectivity event (ID 1360) description for more information

name: Cluster database could not be loaded
knowledgeSummary: The cluster database could not be loaded. Ensure that a good copy of the cluster configuration is available to the node
knowledgeCause: The file may be missing or corrupt
knowledgeResolution: Automatic repair might be attempted
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (1057) description for more information

name: Cluster service cannot be started
knowledgeSummary: The Cluster service cannot be started. An attempt to read configuration data from the Windows registry failed
knowledgeCause: When the cluster configuration on a node is missing or corrupt, the Cluster service cannot load the configuration and therefore cannot start. Where possible, the Cluster service will obtain the latest cluster configuration from other nodes in the cluster. Ensure that other nodes are started
knowledgeResolution: Please use the Failover Cluster Management snap-in to ensure that this machine is a member of a cluster. If you intend to add this machine to an existing cluster use the Add Node Wizard. Alternatively, if this machine has been configured as a member of a cluster, it will be necessary to restore the missing configuration data that is necessary for the Cluster Service to identify that it is a member of a cluster. Perform a System State Restore of this machine in order to restore the configuration data
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (ID 1090) description for more information

name: Failed to unload failover cluster database
knowledgeSummary: The failover cluster database could not be unloaded
knowledgeCause: Sometimes when the node attempts to unload the cluster configuration database, the action does not fully complete
knowledgeResolution: If restarting the cluster service does not fix the problem, please restart the machine
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (ID 1574) description for more information

name: Cluster configuration data is missing or corrupt
knowledgeSummary: An attempt to forcibly start the cluster service has failed because the cluster configuration data on host node is either missing or corrupt
knowledgeCause: Cluster configuration data on host node is either missing or corrupt
knowledgeResolution: Please first start the cluster service on another node that has an intact and valid copy of the cluster configuration data. Then, reattempt the start operation on this node (which will attempt to obtain updated valid configuration information automatically). If no other node is available, please use WBAdmin.msc to perform a System State Restore of this node in order to restore the configuration data
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (ID 1575) description for more information

name: Failed to copy cluster configuration data file
knowledgeSummary: The restore request for the cluster configuration data failed to make a copy of the existing cluster configuration data file (ClusDB)
knowledgeCause: While attempting to preserve the existing configuration, the restore operation was unable to create its copy. This might be expected if the existing configuration data file was corrupt. The restore operation has continued but attempts to revert to the existing cluster configuration may not be possible
knowledgeResolution: Check for other failover cluster events that are related to this event. Also check the System or other logs for additional disk or file system events that might indicate a problem with copying files. If you do not currently have Event Viewer open, see "Opening Event Viewer and viewing events related to failover clustering."
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (ID 1581) description for more information

name: Cluster configuration database cannot be unloaded
knowledgeSummary: The failover cluster database could not be unloaded and any potentially incorrect changes in memory could not be discarded. The cluster service will attempt to repair the database by retrieving it from another cluster node
knowledgeCause: Hosted Services and Applications are might be offline or not functioning correctly
knowledgeResolution: If the cluster service does not come online, restart the cluster service on this node. If restarting the cluster service does not fix the problem, please restart the machine. If the cluster service fails to come online after a reboot, please restore the cluster database from the last backup. Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your cluster configuration and verify that the hosted Services and Applications are online and functioning correctly
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Failover Cluster Configuration Availability event (ID 1593) description for more information

name: Cluster service shut down
knowledgeSummary: The Cluster service is shutting down because quorum was lost
knowledgeCause: This could be due to the loss of network connectivity between some or all nodes in the cluster, or a failover of the witness disk
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapter. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Quorum and Connectivity Needed for Quorum event (ID 1177) description for more information

name: Cluster service fatal error
knowledgeSummary: Cluster service suffered an unexpected fatal error
knowledgeCause: There are various software or hardware related causes that can prevent the Cluster service from starting on a node. Sometimes the Cluster service can restart successfully after it has been interrupted by one of those causes
knowledgeResolution: Review the event logs for indications of the problem
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1000) description for more information

name: Cluster service interruption
knowledgeSummary: Cluster service was halted due to incomplete connectivity with other cluster nodes
knowledgeCause: Cluster service might not be running on all nodes
knowledgeResolution: Check network hardware and configuration of your cluster. Additionally verify that the Cluster service is started on all the nodes in a failover cluster
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1006) description for more information

name: Inconsistency within the failover cluster
knowledgeSummary: The Cluster service was halted to prevent an inconsistency within the failover cluster
knowledgeCause: There are various software or hardware related causes that can prevent the Cluster service from starting on a node. Sometimes the Cluster service can restart successfully after it has been interrupted by one of those causes
knowledgeResolution: Review the event logs for indications of the problem. A recommended order for troubleshooting is as follows: Hardware, Operating system, Networking, Security, Cluster service, Clustered applications or services
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1073) description for more information

name: Cluster service failed to start
knowledgeSummary: The Cluster service failed to start because it was unable to register interface with the RPC service
knowledgeCause: There are various software or hardware related causes that can prevent the Cluster service from starting on a node. Sometimes the Cluster service can restart successfully after it has been interrupted by one of those causes
knowledgeResolution: Review the event logs for indications of the problem. A recommended order for troubleshooting is as follows: Hardware, Operating system, Networking, Security, Cluster service, Clustered applications or services
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1105) description for more information

name: Unexpected cluster service problem
knowledgeSummary: The cluster service encountered an unexpected problem and will be shut down
knowledgeCause: There are various software or hardware related causes that can interfere with the running of the Cluster service on a node. Sometimes the Cluster service can restart successfully after it has been interrupted by one of those causes
knowledgeResolution: Review the event logs for indications of the problem
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Service Startup event (ID 1556) description for more information

name: Cluster network is down
knowledgeSummary: Cluster network is down
knowledgeCause: None of the available nodes can communicate using this network
knowledgeResolution: Run the Validate a Configuration wizard to check your network configuration. If the condition persists, check for hardware or software errors related to the network adapter. Also check for failures in any other network components to which the node is connected such as hubs, switches, or bridges
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Network Connectivity event (ID 1130) description for more information

name: Failed to assign dynamic MAC address
knowledgeSummary: Dynamic MAC address for VM network adapter was not assigned
knowledgeCause: Each host has a pool of MAC Addresses. By default the Pool holds 256 Addresses, and when asked gives them out in a Round Robin fashion. Every time a VM is started, the dynamic MAC address assigned to the NIC is checked against this pool. If the MAC is outside of this pool, a new MAC is taken from the Pool and assigned to the NIC. Again, this check is done during VM startup only. Unfortunately, your MAC Pool might get exhausted earlier when you frequently restart the VMs on a different Host, due to this check
knowledgeResolution: The virtual machine will not have network connectivity until this problem is resolved. To assign a new MAC address, restart the virtual machine

name: Unexpected VM error
knowledgeSummary: This VM has encountered an unexpected error
knowledgeCause: This is a generic error message, please review corresponding event message for more details
knowledgeResolution: Please review corresponding event message for more details on the resolution

name: Insufficient disk space
knowledgeSummary: Hyper-V disk space is low on available free space
knowledgeCause: Disk space on your Hyper-V server is about to run out, please check the disk space where you store the VMs
knowledgeResolution: Increase disk capacity or free up some space by migrating VMs to another datastore

name: Datastore read latency
knowledgeSummary: This host local disk has exceeded the threshold for total read latency. This performance monitor counter measures the amount of time that read operations take to respond to the operating system
knowledgeCause: As a general rule of thumb, average response times greater than 15ms are considered sub-optimal. This is based on the typical seek time of a single 7200 RPM disk drive without cache. Physical disks installed on a non-virtualized environment offer better performance than disks accessed through a Hyper-V host operating system
knowledgeResolution: View the host disk latency for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider using SCSI controllers for the VMs running on this host as they can reduce CPU overhead and enable a virtual SCSI bus to provide multiple transactions simultaneously. Be aware that not all guest OS supports that configuration. If that's not possible, add additional SCSI-connected VHDs (preferably ones that are stored on separate physical spindles or arrays on the host server). Also if you have any concurrently running resource intensive tasks (backup jobs, antivirus checks etc.), consider using different time schedule in order to offload you host disks

name: Datastore write latency
knowledgeSummary: This host disk has exceeded the threshold for total write latency. This performance monitor counter measures the amount of time that write operations take to respond to the operating system
knowledgeCause: As a general rule of thumb, average response times greater than 15ms are considered sub-optimal. This is based on the typical seek time of a single 7200 RPM disk drive without cache. Physical disks installed on a non-virtualized environment offer better performance than disks accessed through a Hyper-V host operating system
knowledgeResolution: View the host disk latency for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider using SCSI controllers for the VMs running on this host as they can reduce CPU overhead and enable a virtual SCSI bus to provide multiple transactions simultaneously. Be aware that not all guest OS supports that configuration. If that's not possible, add additional SCSI-connected VHDs (preferably ones that are stored on separate physical spindles or arrays on the host server). Also if you have any concurrently running resource intensive tasks (backup jobs, antivirus checks etc.), consider using different time schedule in order to offload you host disks

name: Cluster service failed to write data to a file
knowledgeSummary: Cluster service could not write to a file. In a failover cluster, most clustered services or applications use at least one disk, also called a disk resource, that you assign when you configure the clustered service or application. Clients can use the clustered service or application only when the disk is functioning correctly
knowledgeCause: The disk may be low on disk space, or some other serious condition exists
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that the disk has enough free space. If the disk is full, then create more free space or undo the process that is consuming too much disk space. If the disk has enough free space, the file system may be corrupted. If this appears to be the case, run Chkdsk /f on the disk
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's Cluster Storage Functionality event (ID 1080) description for more information

name: High memory usage
knowledgeSummary: There is high utilization of memory within this Virtual Machine. The memory active metric is the current percentage of memory active vs. memory maximum for this VM
knowledgeCause: High demand for memory from workload within the VM. Note that this metric is a more accurate view of what percentage of allocated physical memory is being used by this VM exclusively, as this metric excludes memory shared with other VMs by TSP (transparent page-sharing), where common pages are shared between VMs. Therefore this metric is a more accurate reflection of VM memory usage than what is seen in task manager within the guest OS (which will usually show a higher value)
knowledgeResolution: Check memory usage by OS, applications and/or services within this VM. If this high memory usage is expected, consider increasing the memory allocated to this VM. Check that VMs of common OS and application are running on the same ESX Host, where possible - this will maximize the benefits of TPS (transparent page-sharing) and allow ESX to make most efficient use of physical memory. You should examine related metrics such as memory shared, memory balloon and memory Swap Rate (available at both VM and Host level). Also relevant could be memoryPressure at Host level, which factors in memory overcommitment and also TPS

name: Veeam ONE license expiration date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE license has expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE license has expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to renew your license key

name: Veeam ONE support expiration date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE prepaid support contract has expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE prepaid support contract has expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to renew your support contract
knowledgeExternal: To renew your support contract please follow this link

name: Datastore read latency
knowledgeSummary: Datastore latency has exceeded the threshold of total read latency
knowledgeCause: Response times for read operations for this datastore in the last interval has exceeded the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: View datastore read latency for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider offloading some of the most IO-intensive VMs to other datastores. Check out hosts HBA performance that are connected to this datastore

name: Datastore write latency
knowledgeSummary: Datastore latency has exceeded the threshold of total write latency
knowledgeCause: Response times for write operations for this datastore in the last interval has exceeded the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: View datastore write latency for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider offloading some of the most IO-intensive VMs to other datastores. Check out hosts HBA performance that are connected to this datastore

name: Backup repository free space
knowledgeSummary: Backup repository is low on free space
knowledgeCause: Backup repository free space value dropped below the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: Increase backup repository capacity or alternatively reconfigure the retention policy settings for the backup jobs targeted to the backup repository

name: Backup repository connection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication server lost connection to the backup repository
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication may lose connection to the backup repository due to one of the following reasons: Failure of the network cable, disconnect of the cable, failure of the physical network card, backup repository services failure, firewall issues, backup repository power state
knowledgeResolution: Verify that backup repository is accessible by both backup proxy and Veeam Backup & Replication servers. Check firewall settings on the proxy server (if applicable) and make sure Veeam backup repository services are up and running

name: Backup proxy connection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication server has lost connection to the backup proxy server
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication server may lose connection to the backup proxy server due to the following reasons: network-related issues, firewall-related issues, issues with the backup proxy services, hardware failure or failure of the virtual infrastructure components that host the backup proxy component
knowledgeResolution: Verify that backup proxy is running and accessible for the Veeam Backup & Replication server and vice versa. Check the firewall settings on the backup proxy server (if applicable) and make sure proxy services on the backup server are up and running

name: Veeam Backup & Replication server connection failure
knowledgeSummary: Connection to Veeam Backup & Replication server failed. Be aware that connection absence for more than 1 week for Veeam Backup & Replication server v12.0 and earlier 3 weeks for Veeam Backup & Replication server v12.1 can cause data loss issues.
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE failed to connect to Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeResolution: Check WMI service state on the Veeam Backup & Replication server, verify firewall settings and make sure Veeam Backup & Replication server is up and running. Verify that Veeam Backup & Replication server can be accessed by Veeam ONE server, and the user account specified in the connection settings has sufficient permissions on the Veeam Backup & Replication server. If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager connection failure
knowledgeSummary: Connection to Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager failed
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE failed to connect to Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager
knowledgeResolution: Check firewall settings and make sure Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager is up and running. Verify that Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager can be accessed by Veeam ONE server and user account specified in the connection settings has sufficient permissions on the Veeam Backup Enterprise Manager server

name: Backup job state
knowledgeSummary: Backup job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Backup job reported either an error or a warning which may be potentially indicating that one or more VMs have not been successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the backup job, open Veeam Backup & Replication console and navigate to Home view and select the Jobs node. Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Replication job state
knowledgeSummary: Replication job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Replication job reported either an error or a warning which may indicate that one or more objects have not been successfully replicated
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the replication job: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to Home view and select the Jobs node. Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics. You can also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you provide all the relevant log files when creating a ticket to ensure that all information required for analysis is available to the support team. This will speed up the investigation process and decrease resolution time.

name: SureBackup job state
knowledgeSummary: One or more VMs or computers couldn't be successfully verified
knowledgeCause: Veeam SureBackup job failed to verify one or more VMs or computers that resulted in the entire SureBackup job failure
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows you to generate HTML reports with statistics on a performed SureBackup job, a separate job session and multiple jobs sessions. The SureBackup job report contains data on all sessions initiated for a specific job. To make up a report on the failed SureBackup job: Open the Home view. Click the Jobs node in the inventory pane. Right-click the necessary SureBackup job in the working area and select Report. Also use SureBackup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team.

name: License expiration date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication license expired
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication license key has expired
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to renew your license key

name: Support expiration date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication prepaid support contract expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication prepaid support contract has expired or is going to expire soon
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to renew your support contract
knowledgeExternal: To renew your support contract please follow this link

name: VM consolidation needed status
knowledgeSummary: When initiating Delete or DeleteAll operations on snapshots, the snapshot details are deleted from Snapshot Manager, then the snapshot files are consolidated and merged to another snapshot file or to the virtual machine parent disk. If the consolidation fails, there were no snapshots shown in the Snapshot Manager, but the snapshot files were still being used on the datastore. This can cause the datastore to run out of space
knowledgeCause: Some of the snapshot files failed to consolidate with VM parent virtual disk files
knowledgeResolution: To resolve that you need to initiate a consolidate operation from the vSphere Client or via the ESX command-line
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Consolidating snapshots in vSphere 5 for more information

name: Non VI workload detected
knowledgeSummary: A potential misconfiguration or I/O performance issue caused by a non-ESX workload has been detected. This alarm is triggered when Storage I/O Control (SIOC) detects that a workload that is not managed by SIOC is contributing to I/O congestion on a datastore that is managed by SIOC
knowledgeCause: The list of situations that can trigger this alarm: The host is running in an unsupported configuration. The storage array is performing a system operation such as replication or RAID reconstruction. VMware Consolidated Backup or vStorage APIs for Data Protection are accessing a snapshot on the datastore for backup purposes. The storage media (spindles, SSD) on which this datastore is located is shared with volumes used by non-vSphere workloads
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that you are not running one of the configurations below: One or more hosts accessing the datastore are running an ESX version older than 4.1. One or more hosts accessing the datastore are not managed by vCenter Server. Not all of the hosts accessing the datastore are managed by the same vCenter Server. The storage media (spindles, SSD) where this datastore is located are shared with other datastores that are not SIOC enabled. Datastores in the configuration have multiple extents.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to External I/O workload detected on shared datastore running Storage I/O Control (SIOC) for congestion management in vSphere 5 for more information

name: Non VI workload detected on host
knowledgeSummary: A potential misconfiguration or I/O performance issue caused by a non-ESX workload has been detected. This alarm is triggered when Storage I/O Control (SIOC) detects that a workload that is not managed by SIOC is contributing to I/O congestion on a datastore that is managed by SIOC
knowledgeCause: The list of situations that can trigger this alarm: The host is running in an unsupported configuration. The storage array is performing a system operation such as replication or RAID reconstruction. VMware Consolidated Backup or vStorage APIs for Data Protection are accessing a snapshot on the datastore for backup purposes. The storage media (spindles, SSD) on which this datastore is located is shared with volumes used by non-vSphere workloads.
knowledgeResolution: Ensure that you are not running one of the configurations below: One or more hosts accessing the datastore are running an ESX version older than 4.1. One or more hosts accessing the datastore are not managed by vCenter Server. Not all of the hosts accessing the datastore are managed by the same vCenter Server. The storage media (spindles, SSD) where this datastore is located are shared with other datastores that are not SIOC enabled. Datastores in the configuration have multiple extents.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to External I/O workload detected on shared datastore running Storage I/O Control (SIOC) for congestion management in vSphere 5 for more information

name: ESX(i) host network uplink problems
knowledgeSummary: Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) is included in IEEE specification as a method to control the bundling of several physical ports together to form a single logical channel. LACP allows a network device to negotiate an automatic bundling of links by sending LACP packets to the peer (directly connected device that also implements LACP)
knowledgeCause: Uplink on VDS has been disconnected or blocked
knowledgeResolution: Check the corresponding event for further details on why uplink has been disconnected or blocked

name: ESX(i) host  network uplink failure
knowledgeSummary: Link Aggregation Control Protocol (LACP) is included in IEEE specification as a method to control the bundling of several physical ports together to form a single logical channel. LACP allows a network device to negotiate an automatic bundling of links by sending LACP packets to the peer (directly connected device that also implements LACP)
knowledgeCause: Uplink on VDS is either inactive or the uplink speed has degraded
knowledgeResolution: Check the local duplex setting and verify that the peer switch setting is full duplex. Check the local speed setting on the uplink as well as the peer switch. Check if any of the uplinks are in other lists than active

name: iSCSI target storage connection failure
knowledgeSummary: The iSCSI initiator is unable to establish a connection to the target
knowledgeCause: No targets from an array are seen by: All of the ESX hosts All of the ESX hosts on a specific switch or connected through an uplink One ESX host Targets on the array are visible but one or more LUNs are not: iSCSI LUN not visible iSCSI LUN cannot connect Connectivity issues to the storage array LUN is missing
knowledgeResolution: To resolve connectivity errors: Verify if the iSCSI initiator configuration is correct. Verify if the array is operational/certified. To verify if the array is compatible, see the VMware Compatibility Guide. Verify and ensure that the storage device has a default gateway defined and the array can ping the host. Verify if you are able to ping or vmkping the array. If port binding is enabled, verify if the configuration is correct and try unbinding and binding vmkernel port. For example, the IP address of the storage array and port bound vmknic IP address MUST both be on the same subnet.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Troubleshooting iSCSI array connectivity issues in VMware ESX/ESXi (multiple versions) for more information

name: iSCSI targets are permanently removed from ESXi
knowledgeSummary: The esx.problem.storage.iscsi.target.permanently.removed message is received when an iSCSI target is no longer presented to ESXi
knowledgeCause: This might occur when an iSCSI target that is used as a storage device on the ESXi host is unmapped or made unavailable by the storage system
knowledgeResolution: Perform the steps necessary to recover the host after an unplanned PDL. See details in Knowledge Base article 2004684.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Troubleshooting iSCSI array connectivity issues in VMware ESX/ESXi (multiple versions) for more information

name: Storage ATS support failure
knowledgeSummary: In a shared storage environment, when multiple hosts access the same VMFS datastore, specific locking mechanisms are used. These locking mechanism prevent multiple hosts from concurrently writing to the metadata and ensure that no data corruption occurs. VMFS supports SCSI reservations and atomic test and set (ATS) locking. For storage devices that support hardware acceleration, VMFS uses the ATS algorithm, also called hardware assisted locking. In contrast with SCSI reservations, ATS supports discrete locking per disk sector
knowledgeCause: VAAI uses Atomic Test & Set (ATS) feature during creation and locking of files on the VMFS volume. Please investigate the corresponding event description for potential reasons of the ATS support failure
knowledgeResolution: VAAI hardware offload cannot be used when: The source and destination VMFS volumes have different block sizes The source file type is RDM and the destination file type is non-RDM (regular file) The source VMDK type is eagerzeroedthick and the destination VMDK type is thin The source or destination VMDK is any sort of sparse or hosted format Cloning a Virtual Machine that has snapshots (or doing a View replica or recompose), since this process involves consolidating the snapshots into the virtual disks of the target Virtual Machine. The logical address and/or transfer length in the requested operation are not aligned to the minimum alignment required by the storage device (all datastores created with the vSphere Client are aligned automatically) The VMFS datastore has multiple LUNs/extents spread across different arrays Hardware cloning between arrays (even if within the same VMFS datastore) does not work.

name: vSphere Distributed Switch MTU mismatch
knowledgeSummary: A larger MTU (maximum transmission unit) bring greater efficiency because each packet carries more user data while protocol overheads; the resulting higher efficiency means a slight improvement in bulk protocol throughput. A larger MTU also means processing of fewer packets for the same amount of data. However, this gain is not without some downside. Large packets can occupy a slow link for some time, causing greater delays to following packets and increasing lag and minimum latency
knowledgeCause: The value of MTU configured in the vSphere Distributed Switch mismatches physical switch
knowledgeResolution: Change the MTU value for the vSphere distributed switch to match physical switch MTU configuration

name: Network rollback detected
knowledgeSummary: In vSphere 5.1, rollback is enabled by default. However, you can enable or disable rollbacks at the vCenter Server level. Several networking events can trigger a rollback. The events are grouped into these categories: Host networking rollbacks (virtual switches or network system) Distributed switch rollbacks
knowledgeCause: Host networking rollbacks can occur when an invalid change is made to the host networking configuration. Every network change that disconnects a host also triggers a rollback. Distributed switch rollbacks can occur when invalid updates are made to distributed switch-related objects, such as distributed switches, distributed port groups, or distributed ports
knowledgeResolution: vSphere 5.1 and later allows you to rollback to previous networking configurations if a networking misconfiguration occurs. vSphere 5.1 also allows you to recover from any misconfiguration by connecting directly to a host to fix any networking issues through the Direct Console User Interface (DCUI). Rollback is available for use on both standard and distributed switches
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Understanding vSphere 5.1 network rollback and recovery for more information

name: Teaming mismatch error
knowledgeSummary: The teaming configuration of the uplink ports in the DVS does not match physical switch configuration
knowledgeCause: If the physical switch is configured for EtherChannel (or Link Aggregation Group), please set the portgroup to use a teaming policy of IP Hash. The Health Check will identify when a mismatch occurs
knowledgeResolution: Verify that teaming configuration of the uplink ports in the DVS matches physical switch configuration

name: Uplink port MTU error
knowledgeSummary: MTU health check status of an uplink port is changed
knowledgeCause: In the latest mtu health check, not all the vlans' MTU setting on physical switch allows vSphere Distributed Switch max MTU size packets passing
knowledgeResolution: Verify that MTU settings on physical switch allow vSphere DVS max MTU size packets passing

name: Uplink port VLAN error
knowledgeSummary: Vlans health check status of an uplink port is changed
knowledgeCause: In the latest vlan health check, not all the vlans are trunked by the physical switch connected to the uplink port
knowledgeResolution: Verify that all vlans are trunked by the physical switch connected to the uplink port

name: vCenter storage availability error
knowledgeSummary: The alarm indicates that a VMFS volume on the ESX(i) host is locked due to an I/O error
knowledgeCause: There is a high I/O load on your VMFS volume
knowledgeResolution: Refer to VMFS Volume is Locked KB article for more information

name: ESX(i) host storage error
knowledgeSummary: Storage device becomes permanently lost while SCSI reservation is held by ESXi
knowledgeCause: The device has been removed or is permanently inaccessible while holding a reservation
knowledgeResolution: Power down and unregister any virtual machines that use the inaccessible datastore. Before unmounting the datastore from ESXi, break the device lock by running the following command:vmkfstools -B --breaklock device_path
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Permanent Device Loss (PDL) and All-Paths-Down (APD) in vSphere 5.0 for more information

name: SCSI unsupported plugin warning
knowledgeSummary: An invalid storage module attempted to configure a SCSI device
knowledgeCause: A storage plugin has attempted to allocate resources for a SCSI device. This might be the result of a malfunctioning storage module or a bad configuration
knowledgeResolution: Check your storage configuration and any storage plugin modules on the ESXi host for any issues

name: Connection to iSCSI storage target failure
knowledgeSummary: The iSCSI initiator is unable to establish a connection to the target
knowledgeCause: Failed to establish connection to the iSCSI storage device
knowledgeResolution: To resolve connectivity errors: Verify if the iSCSI initiator configuration is correct. Verify if the array is operational/certified. Verify and ensure that the storage device has a default gateway defined and the array can ping the host. Verify if you are able to ping or vmkping the array. If port binding is enabled, verify if the configuration is correct and try unbinding and binding vmkernel port.

name: ESX(i) host storage failure
knowledgeSummary: An operation on the root file system has failed
knowledgeCause: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this failure
knowledgeResolution: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this failure

name: ESX(i) host storage warning
knowledgeSummary: One of the host's ramdisks reached the limit for the number of files it can contain
knowledgeCause: The file table of the ramdisk 'tmp' is full
knowledgeResolution: If the files were created by administrative action (for example, accidentally copying files to an incorrect path), then remove the files. If the normal operation of the system appears to cause the error, then contact VMware support

name: ESX(i) host CPU hardware error
knowledgeSummary: ESX(i) host has experienced a CPU hardware error
knowledgeCause: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error
knowledgeResolution: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error

name: ESX(i) host network error
knowledgeSummary: DHCP client lease issue has been detected
knowledgeCause: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error
