knowledgeResolution: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error

name: ESX(i) host network warning
knowledgeSummary: DHCP client lease issue has been detected
knowledgeCause: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error
knowledgeResolution: Check the corresponding event description for the reason of this error

name: Isolation addresses is missing
knowledgeSummary: ESX(i) host is missing isolation addresses for isolation detection
knowledgeCause: There is no isolation addresses defined as required by vSphere HA
knowledgeResolution: Refer to Troubleshooting VMware High Availability for more information

name: vSphere cluster HA error
knowledgeSummary: There is an issue with VMware high-availability configuration for this host
knowledgeCause: Review the event description for detail on the cause
knowledgeResolution: Refer to Troubleshooting VMware High Availability for more information

name: vSphere cluster HA warning
knowledgeSummary: There is an issue with VMware high-availability protection for this cluster
knowledgeCause: Review the event description for detail on the cause
knowledgeResolution: Review the event description for detail on the resolution
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Troubleshooting VMware High Availability for more information

name: vSphere cluster warning
knowledgeSummary: HA Cluster operation issue.
knowledgeCause: It can be caused by such events as Host in an HA cluster has been partitioned. HA agent on this host became unreachable. Host in an HA cluster has been isolated. Issue with vSphere High Availability configuration for this cluster.
knowledgeResolution: See VMware online documents such as Troubleshooting VMware High Availability and the vSphere Troubleshooting Guide to resolve HA issues.
knowledgeExternal: Refer to the online vSphere Availability Guide for further guidance.

name: VM disk consolidation failure
knowledgeSummary: There is an issue with the disk for this virtual machine
knowledgeCause: Consolidation has failed
knowledgeResolution: Review the event description for detail on the cause

name: VM HA error
knowledgeSummary: vSphere HA has failed to restart after a host isolation
knowledgeCause: Review the exact description to establish the cause of the event
knowledgeResolution: Review the exact description to establish the cause of the event

name: Host connection failure
knowledgeSummary: This alarm monitors Hyper-V host connection state
knowledgeCause: This host may be disconnected due to manual intervention or failure of the hardware such as failures of the network cable, disconnect of the cable, failure of the physical network card, switch etc
knowledgeResolution: Check Hyper-V host status via Hyper-V manager, verify hosts network availability and configuration. Examine other active alerts against this host in Veeam ONE Client that could indicate a failure. Ensure that host is not due for scheduled downtime or maintenance

name: Too many snapshots on the VM
knowledgeSummary: An excessive number of snapshots in a chain has been detected on the VM which may lead to decreased virtual machine and host performance
knowledgeCause: If you're using a backup tool make sure that snapshots are only present for the duration of the backup process. Snapshots are not backups. As the snapshot file is only a change log of the original virtual disk, do not rely upon it as a direct backup process
knowledgeResolution: Verify the number of snapshots detected on the VM and remove snapshots which are no longer required for this VM
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Best practices for virtual machine snapshots in the VMware environment for more information

name: Cluster memory overcommitment
knowledgeSummary: When placing a virtual machine in a failover cluster, the placement process calculates whether the new virtual machine will over-commit the cluster. If the action will over-commit the cluster, the corresponding alarm will be fired
knowledgeCause: Cluster overcommitment by memory can occur because the sum of free VM slots in the entire cluster is greater than the sum of slots in the largest host (both free and used)
knowledgeResolution: The resolution depends on a variety of factors: The cluster reserve value defined in SCVMM console The amount of memory in each cluster node The amount of memory assigned to each VM The placement of the VMs on nodes in the cluster One of the steps to resolve this issue is to ensure an even distribution of VMs on each cluster node and configure all nodes in the cluster with the same amount of RAM. A more advanced resolution might require considering memory requirements of VMs and placing VMs with similar memory requirements in the same cluster

name: SQL Server Express database size
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE database size is close to maximum database size supported by SQL Server Express edition
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE database size depends on the amount of hosts, VMs and datastores being monitored. For large scale environments it is recommended to use SQL Server Standard or higher
knowledgeResolution: Reduce the number of monitored objects or migrate Veeam ONE database to the SQL Server Standard or higher

name: Maximum allowed job duration
knowledgeSummary: Job has exceeded the maximum allowed job duration threshold configured in the alarm settings
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Performance bottlenecks and connectivity issues Backup proxy servers have an insufficient number of concurrently running tasks allowed Changed Block Tracking (CBT) is disabled in the backup or replication jobs processing vSphere VMs
knowledgeResolution: To investigate the issue, generate historical Veeam ONE reports, such as Job History, and Agent Backup Job and Policy History to get more information about the duration of the jobs. Alternatively, review the details of the latest job session in Veeam Backup & Replication: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics Review the latest job session and check the amount of read and transferred data for each affected workload Additionally, make sure the number of concurrent tasks limit on your backup proxies is configured properly: Navigate to the Backup Infrastructure view and select the Backup Proxies node Right-click the backup proxy in the working area and select Properties For vSphere virtual machine backup and replication jobs, check that CBT is enabled for all VMs in the job: Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Edit Navigate to the Storage tab and click Advanced Navigate to the vSphere tab and make sure the "Use change block tracking data" and "Enable CBT for all protected VMs automatically" checkmarks are set If the increased job run duration is expected and justified, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm thresholds.

name: Latest checkpoint age
knowledgeSummary: The age of the checkpoint (latest) for this VM has exceeded the configured threshold
knowledgeCause: This alarm alerts on virtual machines which have a checkpoint that is older than "X" hours
knowledgeResolution: Make sure checkpoint presence is intentional, otherwise remove the checkpoint from this VM using Hyper-V Manager. Alternatively, the age threshold for this alarm can be changed

name: Latest checkpoint size
knowledgeSummary: The VM has been running on a checkpoint for extended period of time, which exceeded the configured threshold for the checkpoint size. A snapshot preserves the state and data of a VM at a specific point in time
knowledgeCause: A VM provides several operations for creating and managing checkpoint chains. These operations let you create checkpoints, revert to any checkpoint in chain, and remove checkpoints. If a VM is running off of a checkpoint, it is storing all disk changes to a child disk. The more changes are made to the VM disks, the largest the checkpoints file grows
knowledgeResolution: Please check with Hyper-V manager for any open checkpoints running and delete them if needed

name: VMware Cloud Director blocking tasks update failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server failed to update VMware Cloud Director blocking tasks list
knowledgeCause: Network connection to VMware Cloud Director server might not be available
knowledgeResolution: Check firewall and network connection settings to VMware Cloud Director server and try again

name: VMware Cloud Director stranded items update failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server failed to update VMware Cloud Director stranded items list
knowledgeCause: Network connection to VMware Cloud Director server might not be available
knowledgeResolution: Check firewall and network connection settings to VMware Cloud Director server and try again

name: VM total disk latency
knowledgeSummary: Highest latency value across all disks used by the VM
knowledgeCause: Response times for read and write operations for this VM in the last collection interval has exceeded the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: View VM latency for historical information. If your Guest OS workload is disk-intensive, consider relocating this VM to another datastore

name: WAN accelerator connection state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication server lost connection to the WAN accelerator
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication may lose connection to the WAN accelerator due to one of the following reasons: Failure of the network cable, disconnect of the cable, failure of the physical network card, WAN accelerator services failure, firewall issues, WAN accelerator power state
knowledgeResolution: Verify that WAN accelerator is accessible by Veeam Backup & Replication server. Check firewall settings on the WAN accelerator and make sure Veeam WAN accelerator services are up and running

name: vApp runtime lease timeout
knowledgeSummary: vApp runtime lease has expired. Once a vApp is powered on for the first time, the clock starts for the Maximum Runtime Lease. The Maximum Runtime Lease is how long a vApp can be powered on before its automatically suspended.
knowledgeCause: When a runtime lease expires, VMware Cloud Director stops the vApp.
knowledgeResolution: vApp lease time can be reset or reconfigured. To do that navigate to VMware Cloud Director -> My Cloud -> vApps -> Right Click on your vApp and click on the properties option.
knowledgeExternal: Leases provide a level of control over an organization's storage and compute resources by specifying the maximum amount of time that vApps can be running and that vApps and vApp templates can be stored.

name: vApp storage lease timeout
knowledgeSummary: vApp storage lease has expired. A vApp storage lease begins when a user stops the vApp. Storage leases do not affect running vApps.
knowledgeCause: When a storage lease expires, VMware Cloud Director marks the vApp or vApp template as expired, or deletes the vApp or vApp template, depending on the organization policy you set.
knowledgeResolution: vApp lease time can be reset or reconfigured. To do that navigate to VMware Cloud Director -> My Cloud -> vApps -> Right Click on your vApp and click on the properties option.
knowledgeExternal: Leases provide a level of control over an organization's storage and compute resources by specifying the maximum amount of time that vApps can be running and that vApps and vApp templates can be stored.

name: vApp health status
knowledgeSummary: vApp health status has changed.
knowledgeCause: See vApp's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.
knowledgeResolution: See vApp's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.

name: Organization blocking task number
knowledgeSummary: Some tasks are in a pending state as a result of blocking.
knowledgeCause: There are some pending tasks in the blocking list for the organization.
knowledgeResolution: Click the Manage & Monitor tab in VMware Cloud Director, navigate to the Blocking Tasks tab in the left pane and select an action to execute for the blocking task.

name: Org VDC health status
knowledgeSummary: Org VDC health status has changed.
knowledgeCause: See org VDC's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.
knowledgeResolution: See org VDC's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.

name: Org VDC CPU usage
knowledgeSummary: This organization VDC has exceeded the threshold for CPU Usage
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total CPU utilization of the organization VDC. Total CPU usage is calculated based on the vApps and VMs running in this VDC.
knowledgeResolution: View org VDC CPU usage tab for historical information. Check the allocation of CPU resources of vApps and VMs running in this org VDC. Allocate more CPU resources to the organization if required.

name: Org VDC memory usage
knowledgeSummary: This organization VDC has exceeded the threshold for Memory Usage
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total Memory utilization of the org VDC. Total Memory usage is calculated based on the vApps and VMs running in this org VDC.
knowledgeResolution: View org VDC Memory usage tab for historical information. Check the allocation of Memory resources of vApps and VMs running in this org VDC. Allocate more Memory resources to the organization if required.

name: Org VDC storage usage
knowledgeSummary: This org VDC has exceeded the threshold for Storage Usage.
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total Storage utilization of the org VDC. Total Storage usage is calculated based on the space consumed by the VMs of vApps belonging to the org VDC.
knowledgeResolution: Check the allocation of Storage resources of vApps and VMs running in this org VDC. Allocate more storage resources to Provider VDC used by this org VDC.

name: Provider VDC health status
knowledgeSummary: Provider VDC health status has changed.
knowledgeCause: See Provider VDC's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.
knowledgeResolution: See Provider VDC's recent tasks and events for more information on the health status change.

name: Provider VDC memory usage
knowledgeSummary: This provider VDC has exceeded the threshold for Memory Usage.
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total Memory utilization of the provider VDC. Total Memory usage is calculated based on the resource usage of the VDC of the organizations and org VDCs assigned to this provider VDC.
knowledgeResolution: You can merge two or more provider vDCs into a single provider vDC, combining the resources of all merged provider vDCs, or add more physical resources to this provider VDC.

name: Provider VDC CPU usage
knowledgeSummary: This provider VDC has exceeded the threshold for CPU Usage.
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total CPU utilization of the provider VDC. Total CPU usage is calculated based on the resource usage of the VDC of the organizations and org VDCs assigned to this provider VDC.
knowledgeResolution: You can merge two or more provider vDCs into a single provider vDC, combining the resources of all merged provider vDCs, or add more physical resources to this provider VDC.

name: VMware VM tools state
knowledgeSummary: There is a problem with VMware Tools in this Virtual Machine
knowledgeCause: VMware Tools service is not running or is not up-to-date
knowledgeResolution: Check the status of the VMware Tools service/daemon inside the Guest OS. Even if the service is running, this message indicates it is not communicating with the ESX Host/vCenter Server. Try restarting the service

name: Provider VDC storage usage
knowledgeSummary: This provider VDC has exceeded the threshold for Storage usage.
knowledgeCause: This metric is used to measure the total Storage utilization of the provider VDC. Total Storage usage is calculated based on the resource usage of the VDC of the organizations and org VDCs assigned to this provider VDC.
knowledgeResolution: You can merge two or more provider vDCs into a single provider vDC, combining the resources of all merged provider vDCs, or add more physical resources to this provider VDC.

name: Orphaned VM backup snapshot
knowledgeSummary: This VM is running on the snapshot left by backup or replication job
knowledgeCause: Veeam backup or replication job failed to delete the temporary snapshot it was using to backup the VM. This behavior can be caused by performance issue of the vCenter Server or connection problems to the target ESXi host
knowledgeResolution: Remove the snapshot of this VM using vSphere Client Snapshot Manager

name: Backup Copy job state
knowledgeSummary: One or more objects could not be successfully copied from the backup repository
knowledgeCause: Backup Copy job may fail due to one of the following reasons: Failure of the network cable, disconnect of the cable, failure of the physical network card, backup repository services failure, firewall issues, backup repository power state, lack of free space on the target repository
knowledgeResolution: Verify network connection and check free space on the target repository. Also use backup copy log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Network pool usage
knowledgeSummary: Network pool usage has exceeded the configured threshold for this alarm
knowledgeCause: Network Pool assigned to this VDC does not have any more available networks
knowledgeResolution: Please contact the VMware Cloud Director system administrator to create more networks or reconfigure used network pool

name: Organization blocking task timeout
knowledgeSummary: One or more organization blocking tasks has expired
knowledgeCause: Some pending tasks in the organization did not receive any action
knowledgeResolution: Navigate to VMware Cloud Director and click the Manage & Monitor tab and click Blocking Tasks in the left pane to review expired tasks

name: Backup performance data collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server could not collect performance data from the specified Veeam Backup & Replication or Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server. Performance data includes CPU, memory, disk, and network performance counters.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall and connectivity issues The user account used to connect to the backup server is missing the required permissions Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) is not accessible or WMI namespace has been deleted on the Veeam Backup & Replication or Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server Data corruption issues on the Veeam Backup & Replication and Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 servers The backup server is not running Veeam Backup & Replication, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 and Veeam ONE servers are missing the latest update
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Verify that Veeam Backup & Replication or Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server is up, running, and accessible to Veeam ONE server Make sure that the user accounts specified in the connection settings have sufficient permissions on the Veeam Backup & Replication and Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 servers Make sure that WMI is accessible on the Veeam Backup & Replication and Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 servers. You may use WBEMTEST (wbemtest.exe) to do this. Use Performance Monitor (perfmon.exe) or a similar tool to be sure that CPU, memory, disk, and network data is available on the operating system level Install the latest OS updates on Veeam Backup & Replication, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 and Veeam ONE servers If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Tape job state
knowledgeSummary: One or more VMs, files or unstructured data failed to be transferred to the tape device
knowledgeCause: Backup to tape, file to tape or object storage to tape jobs may fail due to one of the following reasons: failure of the tape device drivers, no free space on the target tape, no available tapes in the destination media pool
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the file backup job: Open Veeam Backup & Replication Console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics Verify that the tape device is properly installed on your backup server, tapes are available in the media pool used by the job, there is enough free space on the target tape. If the issue persists, collect file backup job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: File copy job state
knowledgeSummary: One or more files failed to be transferred to the destination folder
knowledgeCause: File copy job could not transfer specified files. This resulted in the entire job warning or failure
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time stentirecs on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. Make sure that network connection to destination folder is available, you have sufficient write permissions and there is enough free space in the target folder

name: Quick Migration job state
knowledgeSummary: One or more VMs failed to migrate to another host
knowledgeCause: Quick Migration job could not move one or more VMs. This resulted in the entire job warning or failure
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. Verify that target host and datastore is reachable, network connection is available and Veeam connection account has sufficient privileges on the ESXi/Hyper-V hosts

name: Cluster Shared Volume read latency
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume has exceeded the configured threshold of total read latency
knowledgeCause: This time includes how long the IO spend inside CSVFS waiting for its turn, as well as how long CSVFS was waiting for its completion from the underlying components
knowledgeResolution: Review CSV latency performance gprahs for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider moving VMs to another volumes or if you have any concurrently running resource intensive tasks (such as backup jobs or antivirus checks) consider using different time schedule in order to offload your CSV datastore

name: Cluster Shared Volume write latency
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume has exceeded the configured threshold of total write latency
knowledgeCause: This time includes how long the IO spend inside CSVFS waiting for its turn, as well as how long CSVFS was waiting for its completion from the underlying components
knowledgeResolution: Review CSV latency performance gprahs for historical information. If your workload is disk-intensive, consider moving VMs to another volumes or if you have any concurrently running resource intensive tasks (such as backup jobs or antivirus checks) consider using different time schedule in order to offload your CSV datastore

name: Bad Hyper-V username logon attempt
knowledgeSummary: This event records a failed logon by user or third party application. The combination of username, password and permissions is the mechanism by which Hyper-V server authenticate a user for access and authorize the user to perform activities
knowledgeCause: Failed logons are due to no match existing between the provided username and password
knowledgeResolution: Check for some automated process that might be logging in with the incorrect or expired password

name: Hardware sensors collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server failed to collect host hardware information
knowledgeCause: Host hardware sensors information is retrieved via CIM-SMASH protocol, sensors status is defined by the firmware of the host
knowledgeResolution: Verify that CIM server on the ESXi host is up and running and confirm that connection account has sufficient permissions on the host via the vSphere Client (Host -> CIM -> CIM interaction)

name: VMware Cloud Director connection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server failed to collect performance and configuration data from VMware Cloud Director
knowledgeCause: The VMware Cloud Director server may be powered off, not responding due to heavy load or vCenter Server SSO service is not running
knowledgeResolution: Check the health state of the VMware Cloud Director server, verify that connection account has sufficient priveleges and examine other alerts generated by the vCenter Server for more information

name: Host CPU ready
knowledgeSummary: This Host has exceeded the threshold for CPU Ready Percent
knowledgeCause: CPU Ready Time is the time a VM waits in a ready-to-run state to be scheduled access to a (physical) CPU by host. This counter represents the average CPU Ready value for all VMs running on the host
knowledgeResolution: Some CPU Ready Time is expected, as host schedules its shared CPU resources. However, if high CPU Ready Time is seen for a VM, combined with slow response times and general poor end-user experience, it could be that the workload in the specific VM is more highly dependent on CPU response. Consider adjusting CPU shares and/or CPU limits for VMs on this host, or moving VMs to another Host with less CPU-intensive activity

name: Backup proxy version is out-of-date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam backup proxy version does not match the version of the Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeCause: Backup proxy may not be updated because the specified backup proxy server was either not accessible or powered off during the upgrade of the Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeResolution: Initiate the upgrade manually for all backup proxy servers that run an outdated version using Veeam Backup & Replication console: Switch to the Backup Infrastructure tab Navigate to the Backup Proxies node and choose the Out of Date node Right-click the backup proxy and choose Upgrade Follow the instructions of the Components Update wizard If the upgrade still does not succeed and returns an error message, make sure the backup proxy is up, running and accessible for the Veeam Backup & Replication server

name: Backup repository version is out-of-date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam backup repository version does not match the version of Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeCause: The specified backup repository was not updated because it was either not accessible or was disabled during Veeam Backup & Replication server update
knowledgeResolution: Upgrade all necessary repository servers by using Veeam Backup & Replication console -> Backup Infrastructure Tab

name: WAN accelerator version is out-of-date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam WAN accelerator version does not match the version of Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeCause: The specified WAN accelerator was not updated because it was either not accessible or was disabled during Veeam Backup & Replication server update
knowledgeResolution: Upgrade all necessary WAN accelerators by using Veeam Backup & Replication console -> Backup Infrastructure Tab

name: Cluster Shared Volume free space
knowledgeSummary: Cluster Shared Volume is low on available free space
knowledgeCause: Cluster Shared Volume has dropped below the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: Free up some space by migrating VMs to another volumes, delete unused and zombie VMs, check out for orphaned snapshot files

name: Local volume free space
knowledgeSummary: Local volume is low on available free space
knowledgeCause: Local volume has dropped below the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: Free up some space by migrating VMs to another volumes, delete unused and zombie VMs, check out for orphaned snapshot files

name: Cloud repository free space
knowledgeSummary: Cloud repository is low on available free space
knowledgeCause: Cloud repository has dropped below the configured threshold because of the increased number of VMs or VMs data transferred to the repository
knowledgeResolution: Increase user quota to have enough free space for new VM backups or ask to adjust the retention policy of the Veeam backup jobs targeted to the cloud repository

name: Cloud repository lease expiration date
knowledgeSummary: Cloud repository represents a storage quota assigned to the Cloud Connect user
knowledgeCause: Cloud repository lease time is about to expire
knowledgeResolution: If it is required, extend the cloud repository lease time period using Veeam Backup & Replication console

name: Cloud gateway version is out-of-date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam cloud gateway version does not match the version of Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeCause: The cloud gateway was not updated because it was either not accessible or was disabled during Veeam Backup & Replication server update
knowledgeResolution: Upgrade all necessary cloud gateway servers by using Veeam Backup & Replication console -> Backup Infrastructure Tab

name: Cloud gateway connection state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication server lost connection to the cloud gateway
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication server may lose connection to the cloud gateway due to one of the following reasons: failure of the network connection, cloud gateway services failure, firewall issues or server power state
knowledgeResolution: Verify that cloud gateway is accessible by Veeam Backup & Replication server. Check firewall settings and make sure that all required services are up and running

name: Tape server version is out-of-date
knowledgeSummary: Veeam tape server version does not match the version of Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeCause: The specified tape server was not updated because it was either not accessible or was disabled during Veeam Backup & Replication server update
knowledgeResolution: Upgrade all necessary tape servers by using Veeam Backup & Replication console -> Tape Infrastructure Tab

name: Tape server connection state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication server lost connection to the tape server
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication server may lose connection to the tape server due to one of the following reasons: failure of the network connection, remote tape access service failure, firewall issues or server power state
knowledgeResolution: Verify that tape server is accessible by Veeam Backup & Replication server. Check firewall settings and make sure that all required services are up and running

name: Host NIC link status
knowledgeSummary: Host physical network adapter (NIC or vmnic) link status is down
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network cable is disconnected from the switch Switch malfunction or misconfiguration NIC driver-related issues The Admin Status of the NIC is purposely switched down
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Check the status of the NIC link using the ESXi service console Check that the affected NIC is still connected to the switch and configured properly Check that the NIC driver is up to date Make sure the Admin Status of the NIC is switched on Look for additional details of the issue by searching for the word "vmnic" in the VODB log file. The NIC failure may be classified with a failed criteria code. This code explains the reason for the NIC failure. If the affected NIC is expected to have the link down, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm settings to exclude this NIC from the alarm.

name: VM with no backup
knowledgeSummary: This VM has not been backed up within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) interval
knowledgeCause: Veeam backup or replication job for this VM did not complete successfully or there is no job configured for this VM
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or separate job session. Review the latest job state for this VM and click the retry button to create a restore point for this VM. If this VM is not present in any job, then consider configuring new job or add this VM to any existing backup or replication job

name: VM with no backup
knowledgeSummary: This VM has not been backed up within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) interval
knowledgeCause: Veeam backup or replication job for this VM did not complete successfully or there is no job configured for this VM
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or separate job session. Review the latest job state for this VM and click the retry button to create a restore point for this VM. If this VM is not present in any job, then consider configuring new job or add this VM to any existing backup or replication job

name: Job disabled
knowledgeSummary: Job is staying in the disabled state for a longer time than configured in the alarm threshold
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication allows disabling scheduled jobs which may be useful during maintenance windows. If a job stays in the disabled state for a longer time than configured in the alarm threshold, it should be re-enabled.
knowledgeResolution: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console and enable required disabled jobs. If the job is expected to remain in the disabled state, modify the list of excluded objects or adjust the suppression settings in the alarm to minimize the amount of "alarm noise".

name: Host HA agent failure
knowledgeSummary: Usually, such triggers indicate that a host has actually failed, but failure reports can sometimes be incorrect. A failed host reduces the available capacity in the cluster and, in the case of an incorrect report, prevents vSphere HA from protecting the virtual machines running on the host
knowledgeCause: The vSphere HA agent on a host is in the Host Failed state. User intervention is required to resolve the situation. This host state is triggered when the vSphere HA master host to which vCenter Server is connected is unable to communicate with the host and with the heartbeat datastores that are in use for the host. Any storage failure that makes the datastores inaccessible to hosts can cause this condition if accompanied by a network failure
knowledgeResolution: Check for the noted host failure conditions and resolve any that are found

name: Job exceeded backup window
knowledgeSummary: One or more jobs exceeded the configured backup window and have been terminated
knowledgeCause: One or more jobs have not been able to complete their sessions within the allowed time period (backup window) configured in the job settings. This resulted in the job failure.
knowledgeResolution: Enable parallel processing on backup proxy servers and backup repositories. Check the jobs schedule and expand the backup window, if necessary. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Virtual Machine Management service is not running
knowledgeSummary: The service required to manage virtual machines is not running. No virtual machine management operations can be performed
knowledgeCause: The service has been stopped or not configured to start automatically
knowledgeResolution: Use the Services snap-in or sc config command-line tool to reconfigure the service to start automatically
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's The Hyper-V Virtual Machine Management service for more information

name: Host Networking Management service is not running
knowledgeSummary: The Hyper-V Networking Management Service is not configured to start automatically. Virtual networks cannot be managed until the service is started
knowledgeCause: The service has been stopped or not configured to start automatically
knowledgeResolution: Use the Services snap-in or sc config command-line tool to reconfigure the service to start automatically
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's The Hyper-V Networking Management for more information

name: Host Image Management service is not running
knowledgeSummary: The service required to manage virtual storage is not running. No virtual storage management operations can be performed
knowledgeCause: The service has been stopped or not configured to start automatically
knowledgeResolution: Use the Services snap-in or sc config command-line tool to reconfigure the service to start automatically
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Microsoft's The Hyper-V Image Management service for more information

name: Backup data collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server could not collect backup data from the specified Veeam Backup & Replication or Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall and connectivity issues The user account used to connect to the backup server is missing the required permissions Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI) is not accessible or WMI namespace has been deleted on the Veeam Backup & Replication server REST API service is not accessible on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server The backup server is not running
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Verify that Veeam Backup & Replication or Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server is up, running, and accessible to Veeam ONE server Make sure that the user accounts specified in the connection settings have sufficient permissions on the Veeam Backup & Replication and Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 servers Make sure that WMI is accessible on the Veeam Backup & Replication servers. You may use WBEMTEST (wbemtest.exe) to do this. Make sure that REST API service is enabled and accessible on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Object properties data collection failure
knowledgeSummary: This event indicates that the Veeam ONE object properties data collection task didn`t finish successfully
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE object properties data collection task failed to collect data from one of the monitored servers
knowledgeResolution: To investigate the issue, perform the following actions: Open Veeam ONE Client and investigate recent alarms on the Alarm tab on Veeam Backup & Replication and Virtual Infrastructure nodes Open Veeam ONE Web Client, log in and navigate to the Settings menu > Event Logs tab, then click on the Object properties data collection task to find out the cause of the failure Depending on the failed session job type, verify that Veeam ONE Reporting service has access to vSphere Server or Hyper-V Server, and Veeam ONE Monitoring service has access to Veeam Backup & Replication server The user account specified in the server connection settings has sufficient permissions If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: VM guest OS reboot
knowledgeSummary: Virtual Machine was rebooted. This warning is applied only to Windows Server 2012 and Windows Server 2012 R2
knowledgeCause: Virtual Machine was reset by the guest operating system
knowledgeResolution: If this is a desired admin action then no resolution is required. This could be done by any user and service who has sufficient permissions. Note that only if initiated inside Virtual Machine will this be captured. Usage of Shutdown or Reboot from within the Hypervisor will not generate this event

name: VM restart
knowledgeSummary: Virtual Machine was rebooted. This warning is applied only to Windows Server 2012 and Windows Server 2012 R2
knowledgeCause: Virtual Machine was reset by the hypervisor
knowledgeResolution: If this is a desired admin action then no resolution is required. This could be done by any user who has sufficient permissions. Note that only if initiated outside of Virtual Machine from the Hyper-V console will this be captured. Usage of Shutdown or Reboot from within the Virtual Machine will not generate this event

name: VM vCPU time per dispatch
knowledgeSummary: The counter shows the average time spent waiting for a virtual processor to be dispatched onto a logical processor
knowledgeCause: Virtual Machine has exceeded the threshold for CPU Wait Time per dispatch
knowledgeResolution: Decrease virtual CPU/physical CPU ratio. Run Veeam ONE 'Oversized VMs' and 'Idle VMs' reports to check if any vCPUs can be reclaimed. Do not keep other software on your host machine other than virtualization-related roles, because extraneous software on the host will preempt and defer the scheduling of work that the vCPUs want to do, thus driving that number up

name: Host restart
knowledgeSummary: Host operation system has been restarted or shut down
knowledgeCause: This event is written when an application causes the system to restart, or when the user initiates a restart or shut down by clicking Start or pressing CTRL+ALT+DELETE, and then clicking Shut Down
knowledgeResolution: If this is a desired admin action then no resolution is required. The restart is being initiated by user who has sufficient permissions. The system security log can provide details. If you don't know who or what script initiates the event, change the password to stop it

name: Host CPU time per dispatch
knowledgeSummary: The counter shows the average time Virtual Machines running on the host spent waiting for a virtual processor to be dispatched onto a logical processor. More vCPUs on host means more things the dispatcher has to schedule thus wait time raises
knowledgeCause: Hyper-V Host has exceeded the threshold for CPU Wait Time per dispatch
knowledgeResolution: Decrease virtual CPU/physical CPU ratio. Run Veeam ONE 'Oversized VMs' and 'Idle VMs' reports to check if any vCPUs can be reclaimed. Do not keep other software on your host machine other than virtualization-related roles, because extraneous software on the host will preempt and defer the scheduling of work that the vCPUs want to do, thus driving that number up

name: Host Memory Pages Usage
knowledgeSummary: The counter shows the rate at which pages are read from or written to disk to resolve hard page faults. This counter is a primary indicator of the types of faults that cause system-wide delays
knowledgeCause: To resolve hard page faults, the operating system swaps the contents of memory to disk, which negatively impacts performance. A high number of pages per second in correlation with low available physical memory may indicate a lack of physical memory
knowledgeResolution: Run Veeam ONE 'Oversized VMs' and 'Idle VMs' reports to optimize memory allocation. Remove superfluous software. Run maintenance tasks off peak times. If the alarm appears during backup operations, consider Off-host backup infrastructure

name: VM with no replica
knowledgeSummary: This VM has not been replicated within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) interval
knowledgeCause: Veeam replication job for this VM did not complete successfully or there is no replication job configured for this VM
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or separate job session. Review the latest replication job state for this VM and click the retry button to create a restore point for this VM. If this VM is not present in any job, then consider configuring new job or add this VM to any existing replication job

name: VM with no replica
knowledgeSummary: This VM has not been replicated within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) interval
knowledgeCause: Veeam replication job for this VM did not complete successfully or there is no replication job configured for this VM
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or separate job session. Review the latest replication job state for this VM and click the retry button to create a restore point for this VM. If this VM is not present in any job, then consider configuring new job or add this VM to any existing replication job

name: Veeam ONE license update failure
knowledgeSummary: License update failure can take place in number of reasons such as connection failure, invalid identifier, expired contract etc. In case of connection problem and licensing server key generation error, Veeam ONE will retry to update the license key
knowledgeCause: Your license failed to update
knowledgeResolution: Try to update your license manually or contact Veeam sales representatives
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Veeam ONE's online documentation

name: VM backups in cloud repository
knowledgeSummary: Number of VMs stored in the cloud repository is above the defined threshold
knowledgeCause: Managed client has reached the numbed of allowed VM backups in the cloud repository
knowledgeResolution: Decrease the number of VM backups that are copied to cloud repository or adjust the threshold in the alarm settings

name: Disk cache error
knowledgeSummary: Failed to write performance data to the disk cache folder
knowledgeCause: Cache disk is not accessible or cannot be used for retrieving performance data
knowledgeResolution: Check if cache disk is accessible and operates with no errors. Review antivirus settings and add this folder to the exclusion list. Once the accessibility is achieved, restart Veeam ONE services to re-initialize cache. To change disk performance cache path, please use Veeam ONE Settings Utility

name: Backup Copy Job exceeded data transfer window
knowledgeSummary: One or more Backup Copy jobs exceeded defined window and data transfer between source and target backup repositories has been stopped. The backup copy job behavior during the 'prohibited' period of time depends on the length of the synchronization interval: If the synchronization interval is greater than the 'prohibited period', the job will put on hold copying operations and wait for allowed hours. The backup copy job is put to the Idle state If the synchronization interval is smaller than the 'prohibited period', all backup copy job sessions that must run during the 'prohibited period' will be finished with the "Failed" status. During the first synchronization interval on allowed hours, the restore point will be copied to the target backup repository
knowledgeCause: One or more Backup Copy jobs was not successfully finished due to data transfer window exceeding
knowledgeResolution: Check the job schedule, expand the backup copy window for terminated jobs. Use faster network between source and target backup repositories. Leverage WAN acceleration feature. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team
knowledgeExternal: Refer to Veeam Backup & Replication's online documentation for more information

name: VM shutdown by host
knowledgeSummary: Virtual Machine was shut down. This warning is applied only to Windows Server 2012 and newer Windows server versions
knowledgeCause: Virtual Machine was shut down by the hypervisor
knowledgeResolution: If this is a desired admin action, no resolution is required. This could be done by any user and service who has sufficient permissions. Note that only if initiated outside Virtual Machine from Hyper-V console will this be captured. Usage of Shutdown or Reboot from within the Virtual Machine will not generate this event

name: VM shutdown by guest
knowledgeSummary: Virtual Machine was shut down. This warning is applied only to Windows Server 2012 and newer Windows server versions
knowledgeCause: Virtual Machine was shut down by the guest operation system
knowledgeResolution: If this is a desired admin action, no resolution is required. This could be done by any user and service who has sufficient permissions. Note that only if initiated inside Virtual Machine will this be captured. Usage of Shutdown or Reboot from within the Hypervisor will not generate this event

name: Guest disk space
knowledgeSummary: Guest OS volume is low on available guest disk space
knowledgeCause: Guest OS free disk space value has dropped below the configured threshold
knowledgeResolution: Increase disk space and extend guest OS volume, or free up some disk space by removing unused programs or files. For Windows guests, you can use Disk Cleanup Wizard

name: Possible ransomware activity
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE detected suspicious activity on this VM
knowledgeCause: This Virtual Machine had high write rate on datastore along with high CPU Usage which can be caused by ransomware activity
knowledgeResolution: Check if files on VM are encrypted by ransomware. Run up-to-date security software, prevent ransomware propagation, ask for qualified assistance if needed. Restore VM or encrypted files from previous backup in a case the files cannot be repaired. If VM was not affected by ransomware, raise the alarm thresholds

name: Veeam Broker Service state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Broker Service has failed
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication service called "Broker Service" is not started or not working properly
knowledgeResolution: Restart Veeam Broker Service. Type services.msc in the Start Search box, then press ENTER. Right-click on the Veeam Broker Service and restart it. Review Windows Event Log to find out the reason. Contact Veeam support if assistance is needed

name: Possible ransomware activity
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE detected suspicious activity on this VM
knowledgeCause: This Virtual Machine had high write rate on datastore along with high CPU Usage which can be caused by ransomware activity
knowledgeResolution: Check if files on VM are encrypted by ransomware. Run up-to-date security software, prevent ransomware propagation, ask for qualified assistance if needed. Restore VM or encrypted files from previous backup in a case the files cannot be repaired. If VM was not affected by ransomware, raise the alarm thresholds

name: Restore activity
knowledgeSummary: Restore session has been initiated
knowledgeCause: A restore session has been initiated on a Veeam Backup & Replication server
knowledgeResolution: If the restore session looks unexpected or suspicious: Navigate to the Data Protection view In the Backup Infrastructure tree of objects, select the required Veeam Backup & Replication server Switch to the Tasks & Events tab Review the event list to determine which user has initiated the restore session.

name: Veeam ONE Server Load
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE Server load is too high
knowledgeCause: High CPU or Memory usage consumption has been detected. Veeam ONE installation can be affected by one of the following ways due to high resources load: Veeam ONE Client slow response Problems with the proper data collection from the infrastructure objects what can cause alarms delay Veeam ONE services could stuck because of the insufficient resources available to operate
knowledgeResolution: Increase CPU or Memory usage. Check whether Veeam ONE database is located on a separate stand-alone SQL Server. Make sure you are not running another resources consuming products on Veeam ONE server by checking Windows Resources monitor

name: Agent backup job state
knowledgeSummary: Agent backup job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Agent backup job reported either an error or a warning which may be potentially indicating that one or more computers have not been successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the agent backup job, open Veeam Backup & Replication console and navigate to Home view and select the Jobs node. Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Agent backup policy session state
knowledgeSummary: Agent backup policy session reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Agent backup policy session reported either an error or a warning which may be potentially indicating that one or more computers have not been successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed policy and sessions and generating reports with statistics on a policy or a separate job session. To review information about the agent backup policy, open Veeam Backup & Replication console and navigate to Home view and select the Jobs node. Right-click the policy in the working area and select Statistics. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Guest services collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE Monitoring Server failed to collect guest services information
knowledgeCause: Failure might be caused by multiple reasons:Access to VM guest OS is deniedRPC service is unavailableConnection is lost
knowledgeResolution: Verify that VM is availableMake sure you choose correct credentials to the VM guest OSCheck if RPC is available

name: Veeam ONE Reporting service state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE Reporting service has failed
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE Reporting service is not started or not working properly. If this service is stopped, data collection jobs will not run at their scheduled times.
knowledgeResolution: Restart Veeam ONE Reporting service. Type services.msc in the Start Search box, then press Enter. Right-click on the Veeam ONE Reporting Server service and restart it. Review Windows Event Log to find out the reason. Contact Veeam support if assistance is needed

name: Guest processes collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE Monitoring Server failed to collect guest processes information
knowledgeCause: Failure might be caused by multiple reasons:Access to VM guest OS is deniedRPC service is unavailableConnection is lost
knowledgeResolution: Verify that VM is availableMake sure you choose correct credentials to the VM guest OSCheck if RPC is available

name: Backup job failed to create storage snapshot
knowledgeSummary: Integrated storage failed to create storage snapshot initiated by Veeam backup job
knowledgeCause: Possible reasons of storage snapshot failure include Wrong credentials Snapshot number limit is reached Lack of free space for the snapshot
knowledgeResolution: Check error message text to find out the reason. Find error details in the log files of integrated storage and VBR server

name: VM power status
knowledgeSummary: VM power state has been changed
knowledgeCause: Power state may change by various of reasons including manually performed shut down by the guest operating system, system restart etc.
knowledgeResolution: Check the Tasks & Events tab to find out why virtual machine was powered off.f VM can be powered on in several ways: Select Start from the Actions pane Select the Start option from the right-click menu Run the powershell command: Start-VM -Name <virtual machine name>

name: Backup repository ReFS data integrity issue
knowledgeSummary: ReFS-formatted volume of a backup repository has experienced a data integrity issue
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE relies on the following events to discover ReFS data integrity issues on ReFS-formatted volumes of backup repositories: Data corruption (event ID 133) Inability to mount the volume because the disk is protected from writing (event ID 135) Volume was created in an older version of Microsoft Windows (event ID 136)
knowledgeResolution: Review the Windows event log of the backup repository to isolate the issue and determine which backup files and what workloads are affected. Then make sure health check (storage-level corruption guard) is enabled for the backup jobs that produced the backup files experiencing the issue. If required, adjust the jobs schedule to perform a health check as soon as possible. The health check will detect data corruptions and may help to retrieve the corrupted data blocks directly from the backup source. Alternatively, you can run an active full backup of the affected workloads to have a guaranteed copy of data. Also, check the health state of the backup repository and it's volumes and make sure there are no issues on the operating system, software, and hardware layers

name: Veeam Analytics service on the Veeam ONE server is not running
knowledgeSummary: The service is required to collect data from the remote servers. When the service is down, no data will be collected.
knowledgeCause: The service has been stopped, is not configured to start automatically, or is not working properly.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the Veeam Analytics service is up and running. Type services.msc in the Start Search box, then press ENTER. Right-click on the Veeam Analytics service and start it. Review the Windows Event Log to find out the reason.

name: Veeam Analytics service on the remote server is not running
knowledgeSummary: The service is required to collect data from the remote servers. When the service is down, no data from this server will be collected.
knowledgeCause: The service has been stopped, is not configured to start automatically, or is not working properly.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the Veeam Analytics service is installed, up and running. For Windows, type services.msc in the Start Search box, then press ENTER. Right-click on the Veeam Analytics service and start it. Review the Windows Event Log to find out the reason. For Linux, verify the service status and start it using the system's service management utilities. Review the system logs to identify any issues. Make sure that the used port is not blocked by the firewall and essential permissions are provided.

name: Veeam intelligent diagnostics failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE failed to analyze Veeam Backup & Replication server logs
knowledgeCause: Possible reasons include: Veeam Analytics service failed to collect VBR logs Veeam Analytics service failed to analyze VBR logs Veeam Analytics service failed to load logs signatures Veeam PowerShell snapin is not installed on VBR server Archive file has been corrupted
knowledgeResolution: Make sure Veeam Analytics service on the Veeam ONE server and backup server are healthy. Perform the Repair action if possible. Update signatures. Make sure VBR server is not under heavy load

name: Veeam Backup & Replication license compatibility
knowledgeSummary: License installed on the backup server is not compatible with the license installed on Veeam ONE server
knowledgeCause: Incompatibility can be caused by change of VBR or Veeam ONE license
knowledgeResolution: Check if license installed on backup server allows monitoring. Make sure that licenses types are compatible

name: Plug-in backup data collection failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server has failed to collect plug-in backup data provided by Veeam Backup & Replication server. Plug-ins for Microsoft Azure, AWS, Google Cloud Platform, and Nutanix AHV are installed on top of a Veeam Backup & Replication server to extend backup functionality for certain platforms and provide compatibility with Veeam cloud backup solutions.
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE server may fail to collect plug-in backup data provided by Veeam Backup & Replication server with one or more plug-ins installed due to the following reasons: The connection between Veeam ONE and Veeam Backup & Replication server hosting the plug-ins has been lost The connection between Veeam Backup & Replication server and backup appliance has been lost This may happen due to network-related issues, communication issues (certificates availability, services state), firewall-related issues, and so on.
knowledgeResolution: Review the alarm details to determine which components are experiencing the issue. Depending on the outcome make sure that: Veeam Backup & Replication server hosting the plug-ins is up, running, and accessible for Veeam ONE server. Check the backup services state to make sure they are running. The backup appliance is up, running, and accessible for Veeam Backup & Replication server Communication certificates are valid on both Veeam Backup & Replication and backup appliance sides If the issue remains, submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you provide all the relevant log files when creating a ticket to ensure that all information required for analysis is available to the support team. This will speed up the investigation process and decrease resolution time.

name: File backup job state
knowledgeSummary: File backup job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: File backup job reported either an error or a warning which may potentially indicate that one or more files were not successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the file backup job: Open Veeam Backup & Replication Console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics If the issue persists, collect file backup job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Database log backup job state
knowledgeSummary: Database transaction logs have not been backed up successfully
knowledgeCause: Database transaction log backup may fail to complete successfully due to the following reasons: The database log backup job task session has exceeded the transaction log backup interval configured in the backup job settings The user account specified in the guest processing settings is missing the required permissions Network, firewall, and connectivity issues Database log chain disruption caused by a third-party tool performing database log backups in parallel with Veeam Backup & Replication
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, review the error description in the alarm, then review the session statistics and session results in Veeam Backup & Replication console. Depending on the root cause, perform the following actions: Increase transaction log backup interval in the job settings Make sure the user account specified in the guest processing settings has the required permissions Make sure no backup operations initiated by a third-party tool are performed on the source databases If the databases reside on a VM, make sure that RPC requests are able to reach guest interaction proxy (if applicable) If the issue persists, collect database log backup job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Suspicious incremental backup size
knowledgeSummary: The size of the recently created incremental restore point is significantly different from the size of the previously created ones
knowledgeCause: The size of the recently created incremental restore point may be different in comparison with the previously created ones due to the following reasons: Possible ransomware activity A significant amount of data blocks of the workload has been changed since the previous backup job session In case when the backup job targets a repository with the "Use Per-VM Backup Files" option disabled the significant change in the size of the incremental restore point may indicate that the list of workloads added to the backup job has been changed. E.g. some workloads have been removed from the backup job or new workloads have been added to the backup job.
knowledgeResolution: Determine what caused the incremental restore point size to change. You may use the Data Change Rate History, Job History, Agent Backup Job and Policy History, and Workload Protection History reports to get more information about the sizes of the affected workload, backup data, and their changes. Make sure the workload is not affected by ransomware. Perform an antivirus scanning of the workload using up-to-date software with updated virus definitions. If a threat is detected, immediately isolate the workload from the rest of the infrastructure and initiate a complex investigation. You may also perform a restore of the entire workload or restore required files from the previous backup in the case when workload files cannot be accessed due to ransomware encryption. If the changes to the size of the incremental restore point are expected, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm thresholds.

name: Backup Copy RPO
knowledgeSummary: Newly created backup was not copied within the defined interval
knowledgeCause: One or more backups were not successfully copied to the secondary repository within the defined RPO
knowledgeResolution: Check the jobs statistics to find out a bottleneck. Consider expanding RPO if optimizations do not help. Also use backup log files to submit a support ticket. It is recommended that you send the whole content of the logs folders to ensure that overall and comprehensive information is provided to the support team

name: Cloud backup policy session state
knowledgeSummary: Cloud backup policy session has finished with either warning or error. Cloud backup policies protect various cloud workloads using cloud-native data protection technologies of Veeam Backup for AWS, Veeam Backup for Azure, and Veeam Backup for Google Cloud Platform.
knowledgeCause: Cloud backup policy session has finished with either warning or error, which may indicate that backup, snapshot, replication, backup copy, or archive operations of one or several cloud instances have not successfully finished
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed cloud backup policy and generating reports with statistics on a cloud backup policy and a separate cloud backup policy session. To view information about a cloud backup policy: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to Home view, and select the Jobs node Right-click the policy in the working area and select Statistics If the issue persists, collect cloud backup policy log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: CDP SLA compliance
knowledgeSummary: SLA % of one or more workloads processed by CDP policy has dropped below the configured threshold
knowledgeCause: SLA % represents the conformity of the CDP policy processing with the configured RPO period. CDP policy aims to transfer data blocks from the source workload (VM or vApp) to the replica during the RPO period configured in the settings of the CDP policy (15 seconds by default). If some data blocks of a workload have not been transferred successfully during the configured RPO period, SLA % decreases for this workload. This may happen due to the following reasons: Network failure or degradation CDP proxy failure Excessive load on a CDP proxy server Failure of the virtual infrastructure components hosting the source workload or its replica
knowledgeResolution: Make sure that virtual and backup infrastructure components are up, running, and accessible to each other. Check CDP proxy cache consumption to make sure the proxy is not overloaded. Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a CDP policy and generating reports with statistics on a CDP policy or a particular CDP policy session. To review information about the CDP policy: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node, CDP view Right-click a CDP policy in the working area and select Statistics If the issue persists, collect CDP policy log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: CDP proxy cache usage
knowledgeSummary: CDP proxy cache usage has reached the configured threshold
knowledgeCause: High CDP proxy cache usage indicates potential data transfer issues related to degradation of network or issues with virtual infrastructure or backup infrastructure components. This may lead to a decrease of SLA % and CDP policy dysfunctions, especially in case of CDP proxy cache overload
knowledgeResolution: Check the health of the virtual and backup infrastructure components and network involved in the CDP data transfer process. Make sure the network connectivity between the CDP infrastructure components is stable Check if the source and target hosts and clusters are up, running and do not experience any issues Make sure the datastores of the target host have enough free space and do not experience high I/O utilization Make sure CDP proxies have enough allocated resources Increase the CDP Proxy cache size if the currently configured amount is not enough for successful processing of the workloads in the current network conditions

name: Unusual job duration
knowledgeSummary: Job has exceeded its usual duration by more percent than configured in the alarm threshold. The usual job duration is calculated as the average duration of a certain number of job runs. You may change the number of job sessions to be analyzed in the alarm rule settings by changing the "Analysis depth" parameter.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Performance bottlenecks and connectivity issues Backup proxy servers have an insufficient number of concurrently running tasks allowed Changed Block Tracking (CBT) is disabled in the backup or replication jobs processing vSphere VMs
knowledgeResolution: To investigate the issue, generate historical Veeam ONE reports, such as Job History, Agent Backup Job and Policy History, and Workload Protection History to get more information about the duration of the affected job. Alternatively, review the details of the latest job session in Veeam Backup & Replication: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics Review the latest job session and check the amount of read and transferred data for each affected workload Additionally, make sure the number of concurrent tasks limit on your backup proxies is configured properly: Navigate to the Backup Infrastructure view and select the Backup Proxies node Right-click the backup proxy in the working area and select Properties For vSphere virtual machine backup and replication jobs, check that CBT is enabled for all VMs in the job: Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Edit Navigate to the Storage tab and click Advanced Navigate to the vSphere tab and make sure the "Use change block tracking data" and "Enable CBT for all protected VMs automatically" options are selected If the increased job run duration is expected and justified, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm thresholds.

name: Computer with no backup
knowledgeSummary: This computer has not been backed up within the configured RPO (Recovery Point Objective) period
knowledgeCause: There are no restore points created for the computer by Veeam Agent backup job or backup policy within the RPO period configured in the alarm settings
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed backup job and backup policy and generating reports with statistics on a backup job or a separate backup job session. Review the latest session for a computer and click the Retry button to create a restore point for this computer, if necessary. If a computer is not included into any backup job or backup policy, consider configuring a new backup job or a backup policy, or add this computer to any existing backup job or backup policy. If a computer is protected locally, consider excluding the computer from the alarm scope

name: Veeam ONE license exceeded
knowledgeSummary: You have exceeded your license limit, and are no longer compliant with your Veeam licensing agreement.
knowledgeCause: The number of monitored workloads from Veeam Backup & Replication or virtual infrastructure or the number of monitored server sockets does not match the value specified in the license file.
knowledgeResolution: To avoid service interruptions from future growth, we recommend you deploy an updated license key to cover the newly monitored workloads.

name: Veeam ONE email notification delivery failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE server was not able to deliver an automated email notification.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall and connectivity issues The user account specified for the mail server is missing the required permissions
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Verify that mail server is up, running, and accessible from Veeam ONE server Make sure that the user account has the required permissions if the mail server requires authentication If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Veeam ONE server failed to connect to Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server
knowledgeSummary: Connection to Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server failed.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall and connectivity issues The user account used to connect to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server is missing the required permissions REST API service is not accessible on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server The Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server is not running
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Verify that Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server is up, running, and accessible from the Veeam ONE server Make sure that the user account specified in the connection settings has sufficient permissions on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server Make sure that REST API service is enabled and accessible on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Backup proxy connection failure (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server could not connect to the backup proxy
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall and connectivity issues The backup proxy server is not running The backup proxy service is not running
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Verify that backup proxy server is up, running, and accessible to Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server Make sure that Veeam Backup Proxy for Microsoft 365 Service (Veeam.Archiver.Proxy) service is running. If the issue persists, collect Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Backup repository free space (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Backup repository of Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 is getting low on free space
knowledgeCause: The amount of free space in a backup repository of Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 dropped below the threshold configured in the alarm settings
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, increase the capacity of the backup repository or clean up the unnecessary backup data. If the affected backup repository is an object storage, increase the object storage consumption limit configured in the object storage settings. To clean up obsolete data, change the retention policy value in the settings of the backup repository. If required, you may adjust the alarm thresholds in the alarm settings.

name: Immutability state
knowledgeSummary: The immutability setting is either turned off or unavailable on one or more backup repositories or on scale-out backup repository extents
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication provides several capabilities to make the protected data immutable, thus resilient to cyber threats such as ransomware and malicious deletions. These capabilities include: WORM-compliant immutability for Linux Hardened Repositories and Linux performance tier extents of scale-out backup repositories S3 Object Lock immutability for object storage repositories If there are any repositories or scale-out backup repository extents that allow the use of one of the aforementioned capabilities but are not configured to use them, or the immutability setting becomes disabled, this alarm triggers.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the immutability setting of the affected backup repositories is configured according to the internal security policy. To view the configuration settings: In Veeam ONE Client, open the summary tab of the corresponding backup repository or scale-out backup repository extent In Veeam ONE Client or Veeam ONE Web Client, generate the Scale-Out Backup Repository Configuration report To investigate immutability setting changes, generate Backup Infrastructure Audit or Backup Objects Change Tracking reports using Veeam ONE Client or Veeam ONE Web Client and determine by whom and when it was enabled or disabled. If the change is not intentional and an adjustment of the immutability settings is required, perform the following steps.
