For Linux Hardened (Immutable) Repositories: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate the affected repository Depending on the authentication method, perform the steps required to configure the immutability setting accordingly For object storage repositories: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate the affected repository Reconfigure the immutability settings if required Some object storage vendors restrict changing the immutability settings on the bucket. If it's not possible to apply the change from within Veeam Backup & Replication console, contact the technical support of the object storage repository and ask for an investigation. Ask the technical support representatives to adjust the immutability settings of the bucket manually to match the desired configuration. If necessary, refer to the official documentation of Veeam Backup & Replication or open a support case with Veeam Customer Support.

name: Immutability change tracking
knowledgeSummary: The configured immutability period has been changed on one or several backup repositories or scale-out backup repository extents
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication provides several capabilities to make the protected data immutable, thus resilient to cyber threats such as ransomware and malicious deletions. These capabilities include: WORM-compliant immutability for Linux Hardened Repositories and Linux performance tier extents of scale-out backup repository S3 Object Lock immutability for object storage repositories When the immutability period of these repositories changes, this alarm triggers.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the immutability setting of the affected backup repositories is configured according to the internal security policy. To view the configuration settings: In Veeam ONE Client, open the summary tab of the corresponding backup repository or scale-out backup repository extent In Veeam ONE Client or Veeam ONE Web Client, generate the Scale-Out Backup Repository Configuration report To investigate immutability setting changes, generate Backup Infrastructure Audit or Backup Objects Change Tracking reports using Veeam ONE Client or Veeam ONE Web Client and determine by whom and when it was enabled or disabled. If the change is not intentional and an adjustment of the immutability settings is required, perform the following steps. For Linux Hardened (Immutable) Repositories: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate the affected repository Depending on the authentication method, perform the steps required to configure the immutability setting accordingly For object storage repositories: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate the affected repository Reconfigure the immutability settings if required If necessary, refer to the official documentation of Veeam Backup & Replication or open a support case with Veeam Customer Support.

name: Job disabled (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 job is staying in the disabled state for a longer time than configured in the alarm threshold
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup for Office 365 allows disabling scheduled jobs which may be useful during maintenance windows. If a job stays in the disabled state for a longer time than configured in the alarm threshold, it should be re-enabled.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue either enable the job or adjust the alarm configuration. If the job is expected to remain in the disabled state, modify the list of the jobs included in the alarm rule, adjust the alarm suppression settings, or adjust the alarm thresholds. To enable the job perform the following actions: Navigate to Organizations view In the inventory pane, select an organization In the preview pane right-click on the job and select Enable. To change the alarm configuration perform the following actions: Navigate to Alarm Management view Select the alarm from the left pane and on the left, right-click the alarm name and select "Edit...". Navigate to Rules tab to adjust the alarm rule settings Navigate to Supress tab to adjust the supression settings

name: License exceeded for Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 is licensed per protected user account. A user account is considered protected when you have at least one restore point containing the data of that user account created within the last 31 days. User account data includes mailboxes, OneDrive for Business accounts, and SharePoint personal sites. If a user and his data have not been backed up for 31 days, his license gets revoked automatically. When the license is exceeded, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 behaves depending on the license type. Subscription license allows exceeding no more than 10 user accounts or 10% of the license count (whichever is greater). For Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 version 7a with the Update license automatically option enabled, it is allowed to exceed no more than 20 user accounts or 20% of the license count (whichever is greater). If you exceed the license by more than these values, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 allows you to process these extra user accounts according to the FIFO queue logic (that is, "first in ? first out"); no additional accounts are allowed. The grace period in this case is not limited and lasts during the whole term of the subscription. Rental license allows exceeding no more than 20 user accounts or 20% of the license count (whichever is greater). For Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 version 7a with the Update license automatically option enabled it is allowed to exceed no more than 40 user accounts or 40% of the license count (whichever is greater). If you exceed the license by more than these values, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 allows you to process these extra user accounts according to the FIFO queue logic (that is, "first in ? first out"); no additional accounts are allowed. The grace period, in this case, is 2 months. After this period, the processing of excessive user accounts (in the FIFO queue) will be stopped; no extra accounts will be queued for processing. Not For Resale (NFR) and Evaluation licenses do not allow the processing of user accounts that exceed the license count. Important! For all types of licenses, the restore capabilities will continue to function regardless of whether your license has been exceeded or not.
knowledgeCause: You have exceeded the number of users you can protect with your currently installed license
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to get a new Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 license to protect more users or decrease the number of users you are currently protecting and delete unnecessary backups.

name: License expiration date (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Each license key issued for Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 has a defined expiration date. When the license expires, Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 behaves depending on the license type. For Subscription license, a grace period of 1 month is granted after the expiration of the license for purpose of renewal. During this period, the application functionality is not limited. After this period, processing of all user accounts in all organizations will be stopped; scheduled jobs will be terminated with failure. In both cases, a notification message will be shown to notify you that the license is either about to expire or has expired. For Rental license, a grace period of 2 months is granted after the expiration of the license for purpose of renewal. During this period, the application functionality is not limited. After this period, processing of all user accounts in all organizations will be stopped; scheduled jobs will be terminated with failure. In both cases, a notification message will be shown to notify you that the license is either about to expire or has expired. For Not For Resale (NFR) license, within a month before the expiration date, you will receive a notification message stating that your license is about to expire. During this period, the application functionality will not be limited by any means. After your license expires, the processing of all user accounts will be stopped. For Evaluation license, within a month before the expiration date, you will receive a notification message stating that your license is about to expire. During this period, the application functionality will not be limited by any means. After your license expires, the processing of all user accounts will be stopped. Important! For all types of licenses, the restore capabilities will continue to function regardless of whether your license has expired or not.
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 license has expired
knowledgeResolution: Contact Veeam sales representatives to renew your Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 license

name: Unusual job duration (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Job has exceeded its usual duration by more percent than configured in the alarm threshold. The usual job duration is calculated as the average duration of a certain number of job sessions (job runs). You may change the number of job sessions to be analyzed in the alarm rule settings by changing the "Analysis depth" parameter.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Performance bottlenecks and connectivity issues Backup proxy servers have insufficient resources Backup proxy servers configuration limits the throughput
knowledgeResolution: To investigate the issue, generate a historical Veeam ONE report, such as Job History, to get more information about the duration of the affected job. Alternatively, review the details of the latest job session in Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365: Open the History view In the inventory pane, expand the Jobs node Select the job session that caused the alarm to trigger from the list Check the statistical information provided for the job session. Validate the job session duration, the amount of transferred data, and bottleneck. Additionally, consider adjusting the number of threads and network traffic throttling settings of a particular backup proxy server. If the increased job run duration is expected and justified, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm thresholds.

name: Organization with no backup
knowledgeSummary: This Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 organization has not been backed up within the configured RPO (Recovery Point Objective) period. An organization is considered as having no backup when none of the objects of that organization have a restore point created within the RPO period configured in the alarm rule settings.
knowledgeCause: There are no restore points created for the objects of the organization within the RPO period configured in the alarm settings
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the protection state and available restore points of the organization and its objects, generate the Protected Objects report. Alternatively, review the job sessions processing the organization in Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 and run the job to create a new restore point for the organization, if necessary. If the organization is not added into any job, consider configuring a new backup job for this organization, or add it to any existing backup job.

name: Audit log failure
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE could not write an event to the audit log. Veeam ONE uses Microsoft Windows Event Log as a centralized system for storing audit logs and designates the "Veeam ONE" event log for this purpose.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Maximum log size has been reached and the configured audit log settings do not allow overwriting or archiving aging events Event log file is locked by another process Service accounts of Veeam ONE Monitoring service (VeeamDCS.exe) and Veeam ONE Reporting service (VeeamRSS.exe) are missing the required permissions Not enough free space on the Veeam ONE server
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Check that there is free space on the disk of Veeam ONE server where the event log is stored Make sure the event log has not reached its maximum size Check the log properties of the event log and make sure the "Overwrite events as needed (older events first)" or "Archive the log when full, do not overwrite events" option is selected Clean the obsolete events manually, if needed Make sure the Veeam ONE audit log file is not locked by any third-party processes Verify that service accounts of Veeam ONE Monitoring service (VeeamDCS.exe) and Veeam ONE Reporting service (VeeamRSS.exe) have the required permissions assigned If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Audit log free space
knowledgeSummary: The amount of free space dedicated for audit log is getting low. Veeam ONE uses Microsoft Windows Event Log as a centralized system for storing audit logs and designates the "Veeam ONE" event log for this purpose. Microsoft Windows allows setting up a maximum log size to prevent the event log from growing indefinitely. The default maximum log size is 16384 KB and can be changed if required. Also, by default, this log is configured to be overwriting the oldest events with the most recent ones upon reaching the event log capacity limit.
knowledgeCause: The amount of free space in the "Veeam ONE" event log which contains audit events has dropped below the configured alarm threshold
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Increase the size limit of the "Veeam ONE" event log Clean the obsolete events manually, if needed If the event log configuration uses the "Overwrite events as needed (older events first)" or "Archive the log when full, do not overwrite events" option and this approach to preventing the event log from growing indefinitely is acceptable, you may acknowledge the alarm.

name: Backup job state (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Backup job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Backup job session reported either an error or a warning which may potentially indicate that one or several organization objects have not been successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the backup job and its latest session, generate the Latest Job Status report. Alternatively, review the details of the latest job session in Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365: Open the History view In the inventory pane, expand the Jobs node Select the affected job session from the list Check the statistical information provided for the job session. Validate the job session status and messages in the job session action log If the issue persists, collect backup job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Backup copy job state (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Backup copy job reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Backup copy job session reported either an error or a warning which may potentially indicate that restore ponts of one or several organization objects have not been successfully copied to an object storage
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the backup copy job and its latest session, generate the Latest Job Status report. Alternatively, review the details of the latest job session in Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365: Open the History view In the inventory pane, expand the Jobs node Select the affected job session from the list Review the statistical information provided for the job session. Validate the job session status and messages in the job session action log. If the issue persists, collect backup copy job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Reporting data scheduling issue
knowledgeSummary: Report, report folder, or dashboard scheduling task of Veeam ONE Web Client has finished with either error or warning. This may indicate that Veeam ONE cannot deliver one or several scheduled reports, report folders, or dashboards via email or save them to a target folder.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Network, firewall, and connectivity issues Issues with the configured SMTP server The user account used to connect to the SMTP server is missing the required permissions Authentication issues, including those related to OAuth and its components (if applicable) The recipients or target folder configured in the schedule do not exist The user account used to write the scheduled objects to the target folder is missing the required permissions Lack of free space on the underlying storage of the target folder
knowledgeResolution: To investigate the issue, open Veeam ONE Web Client, click Configuration in the top right corner, switch to the Event Logs tab on the left, and apply filters to find the affected scheduling task. Click the scheduling task name and review the event log to determine the cause of the issue. Verify that the connected SMTP server is up, running, and accessible to Veeam ONE server. Make sure that SMTP settings are configured properly: Open Veeam ONE Client In the main menu, select Server Settings On the SMTP Settings tab check the configured settings and click "Send Test Email" Make sure the recipients or target folder configured in the schedule exist Make sure that there is enough free space on the storage of the target folder to save the scheduled objects. If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Application backup policy state
knowledgeSummary: Application backup policy reported either an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Application backup policy reported either an error or a warning which may potentially indicate that one or more databases on this application server or application cluster have not been successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the application backup policy and its data backup task sessions, generate the Workload Protection History report. Alternatively, review the details of the application backup policy sessions in Veeam Backup & Replication: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the application backup policy in the working area and select Statistics If the database is expected to not be backed up, modify the alarm assignment to exclude the desired application servers and clusters from it. To achieve this, you may create a dedicated Business View group that will automatically group the desired application servers and clusters based on configured grouping parameters. If the issue persists, collect application backup policy log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Staging data processing issue
knowledgeSummary: The age of the oldest data record accumulated in the staging data of the Veeam ONE database has reached the threshold configured in the alarm settings. Veeam ONE uses a dedicated "staging" table in its database to process data received from the data collector for further usage in the reporting engine. During the staging data processing, Veeam ONE transforms and enriches the collected data, and creates additional data sets that are convenient for building reports and representing data in various Veeam ONE Web Client views.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Lack of CPU, memory, or free disk space on the Veeam ONE database server The staging data is accumulating faster than it could be processed Data collection had started after being stopped for a long period, which caused a massive data flow Microsoft SQL Server hosting Veeam ONE database has selected a suboptimal execution plan
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, perform the following actions: Make sure that the Veeam ONE database server has sufficient amount of CPU, memory, and free disk space If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Application with no recent data backup sessions
knowledgeSummary: One or several databases on an application server or application cluster have no recent data backup task sessions that finished with success or warning within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) interval. A data backup task session is an entity processing the data files of a single database residing on an application server or application cluster. A data backup task session that has finished with success or warning guarantees the availability of storage that can be used to recover the data of the related database stored in it. When a data backup task session fails, the storage is usually not created, thus preventing the ability to recover the data.
knowledgeCause: Databases on this application server or application cluster have no recent data backup task sessions that finished with success or warning
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the application backup policy and its data backup task sessions, generate the Workload Protection History report. Alternatively, review the details of the application backup policy sessions in Veeam Backup & Replication: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the application backup policy in the working area and select Statistics If the issue persists, collect application backup policy log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Scale-out backup repository data transfer session state
knowledgeSummary: Scale-out backup repository offload, download, archiving, or retrieval data transfer session reported either an error or a warning which may potentially indicate that one or several restore points were not uploaded to or downloaded from capacity or archive tier extents. Offload, download, archiving, and retrieval data transfer sessions are used to transfer data between the performance tier, capacity tier, and archive tier extents of a scale-out backup repository.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Connectivity issues Object storage consumption limit has been reached The user account used to connect to the object storage is missing the required permissions Corruption of the backup files with metadata
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the scale-out backup repository data transfer sessions, generate the Workload Protection History report. Alternatively, review the details of the scale-out backup repository data transfer session in Veeam Backup & Replication: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the History view In the inventory pane, select the Storage management node Select the affected data transfer session from the list Review the statistical information provided for the data transfer session Make sure that the scale-out backup repository extents have network connectivity to the underlying object storage of the capacity and archive tier extents. Make sure that the user accounts specified in the object storage repository settings have sufficient permissions. Rescan scale-out backup repository to remediate possible corruption of the backup files with metadata: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Navigate to the Backup Infrastructure view In the inventory pane, right-click on the affected scale-out backup repository and select "Rescan" If the issue persists, collect Veeam Backup & Replication log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 service state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 service (Veeam.Archiver.Service.exe) is not running on the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 server.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because the service has been stopped manually or could not be started.
knowledgeResolution: Verify that Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 service is running using the Services application (services.msc). Start the service manually if it is not running. If the service cannot be started, try rebooting the server and make sure the third-party software is not preventing the service from starting. If the issue persists, collect Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: ServiceNow instance connection state
knowledgeSummary: Connection to a ServiceNow instance has failed
knowledgeCause: ServiceNow instance might be unavailable
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, follow the steps below: Verify ServiceNow Instance: Ensure that the ServiceNow instance is online by accessing its URL in a web browser. If you are using a proxy or VPN, ensure it is correctly configured and functioning properly. Check Credentials & Permissions: Confirm that the username and password used to connect to the ServiceNow instance are correct. Ensure the account has the necessary permissions to access required services or data. Test Connection: Ping the ServiceNow server address to check if it is reachable. Use native diagnostic tools, such as 'traceroute' or 'telnet' to test the connection to the specified server. If you are unsure of the ServiceNow server's port, the default is usually 80 for HTTP and 443 for HTTPS. Perform Additional Troubleshooting: If there are firewalls or network filters in place, ensure they are not blocking access to the ServiceNow server. Check for any reported outages or maintenance periods for ServiceNow that might be affecting your connection. If the issue still persists, consider reaching out to your IT support team or ServiceNow support for further assistance.

name: Backup server security & compliance state
knowledgeSummary: Security and compliance check finished with warning or error. Veeam Backup & Replication uses a built-in tool to ensure the security best practices for the Microsoft Windows and Linux operating systems and Veeam backup infrastructure components are met.
knowledgeCause: Security and compliance check reported either an error or a warning, which may potentially indicate that the Veeam Backup & Replication configuration does not meet one or more best practices for Microsoft Windows or Linux operating system and Veeam backup infrastructure components.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure that the affected backup server is configured according to best practices and recommendations. To view the configuration settings in the Veeam ONE Web Client, generate the Backup Security & Compliance report. If necessary, refer to the official documentation of Veeam Backup & Replication or open a support case with Veeam Customer Support.

name: Syslog server connection state
knowledgeSummary: Connection to a Syslog server has failed
knowledgeCause: The Syslog server might be unavailable
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, please follow the steps below: Verify Syslog server: Ensure that the Syslog server is online by accessing any known services or interfaces it provides. If you are using a proxy, VPN, or any network filters, ensure they are correctly configured and not causing interruptions. Test connection: Ping the Syslog server address to check if it is reachable. Use native diagnostic tools, such as 'traceroute' or 'telnet' to test the connection. Syslog typically uses port 514 for both TCP and UDP, so ensure these ports are open and reachable. Check configuration settings: Review the configuration settings of the device or application sending logs to the Syslog server. Ensure the target server address and port are specified correctly. Make sure the Syslog server is configured to listen to incoming logs from the source address and that there are no filters blocking the incoming logs. Perform additional troubleshooting: Check if there are firewalls or network filters that can block access to the Syslog server or the specified port. Review Syslog server logs for any error messages or indications of connection attempts. If the issue still persists, consider reaching out to your IT support team or the software provider for further assistance.

name: Veeam malware detection activity state
knowledgeSummary: This event is triggered when inline entropy or file system activity analysis settings are disabled. These settings affect the Veeam Backup & Replication server's capability to perform restore point ransomware checks. The inline entropy analysis feature uses AI/ML to detect previously unencrypted data becoming encrypted in processed disk images, which is often an indication of a ransomware attack. File system activity analysis detects the appearance of suspicious files, the deletion of a large number of existing files, etc.
knowledgeCause: Inline entropy or file system activity analysis is disabled on the backup server
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the Enable inline entropy analysis and Enable file system activity analysis are enabled to generate malware detection events on backup servers. To view the configuration settings: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate to the Inventory view and select Malware Detection node Open settings window If one or both malware detection methods on that Veeam Backup & Replication server are not required, acknowledge the alarm and modify the alarm rules.

name: Potential malware in backups
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication makes the backup system more secure by checking restore points for signs of possible malware. After each backup job finishes, a check is started, looking for malware and any unusual activities that could mean there's a security risk.
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication server has detected a potential malware activity.
knowledgeResolution: Quickly find and separate all systems that are at risk and affected to stop the malware from spreading further. To recover from a malware attack, restore your workloads from a restore point that is marked as clean or is in an OK state. Also, Veeam offers advanced restore options like Instant VM Recovery, Secure Restore, Staged Restore, and SureBackup for getting back restore points in safe environments and doing extra checks for hidden malware. To check the available clean restore points in Veeam ONE Web Client, start the Malware Detection report.

name: Potential infrastructure malware activity
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication strengthens the security of the backup system using advanced tools like inline malware scans. It also provides an API that other services can use to check and mark VMs as suspicious.
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication server has detected a potential malware activity.
knowledgeResolution: Quickly find and separate any systems that might be affected and are at risk to stop the malware from spreading. You disconnect the affected VMs from the production network as an immediate step to mitigate the issue. To recover from a malware attack, restore your workloads from a restore point that is marked as clean or is in an OK state. Also, Veeam offers more advanced restore options like Instant VM Recovery, Secure Restore, Staged Restore, and SureBackup. These options let you recover restore points in safe environments and run more checks for hidden malware. To look at the available clean restore points in Veeam ONE Web Client, start the Malware Detection report.

name: Veeam malware detection exclusions change tracking
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication provides a possibility to exclude specific backup objects from malware detection. Objects specified in the exclusion list will not be processed with the built-in malware detection engine.
knowledgeCause: A workload was added to or removed from the malware detection exclusions list
knowledgeResolution: Review the reported objects and make sure that this change was made on purpose.

name: Object storage backup job state
knowledgeSummary: Object storage backup job reported an error or a warning
knowledgeCause: Object storage backup job reported an error or a warning, which may potentially indicate that one or more buckets were not successfully backed up
knowledgeResolution: Veeam Backup & Replication allows viewing real-time statistics on a performed job and generating reports with statistics on a job or a separate job session. To review information about the object storage backup job: Open Veeam Backup & Replication Console Navigate to the Home view and select the Jobs node Right-click the job in the working area and select Statistics If the issue persists, collect object storage backup job log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all the comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Veeam malware detection change tracking
knowledgeSummary: This event is triggered when malware scan settings are changed. These settings affect the Veeam Backup & Replication server capability to perform restore point ransomware checks.
knowledgeCause: The backup server reported changes in one or more malware scan settings that affect the capability to perform restore point ransomware checks
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the malware scan settings of the affected backup servers are configured properly. To view the configuration settings: Open Veeam Backup & Replication console Connect to the affected backup server Navigate to the Inventory view and select the Malware Detection node Click the Settings button If the change of malware scan settings on the Veeam Backup & Replication server is required and justified, acknowledge the alarm or modify the alarm rules.

name: Restore activity (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: This alarm gives visibility across the restore sessions of specific mailboxes, confidential information stored on SharePoint, and Teams conversations.
knowledgeCause: A restore session has been initiated on a Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 Server
knowledgeResolution: Restore sessions may contain emails, files, or other Microsoft 365 entities. It is essential to ensure that these sessions are expected and authorized. If you find a restore session that seems unexpected or suspicious, follow the steps below to investigate the issue: Run the Restore Operator Activity report: Initiate the Restore Operator Activity report for Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365. Specify the desired time range to narrow down the investigation period. Analyze the report: Identify who initiated the restores. This will help in verifying the legitimacy of the operations. Review the specific objects that were restored and their respective targets to understand the scope of the activity. Take appropriate actions: If unauthorized activity is detected, consider revoking the rights of the involved operators and escalating the issue to the IT security team. Ensure to monitor future restore activities closely to prevent unauthorized actions. Always keep track of restore sessions and maintain a secure backup and restore environment to safeguard your Microsoft 365 data.

name: Veeam ONE database cleanup task status
knowledgeSummary: The cleanup task queue appears when the object information is deleted faster than the Veeam ONE server can process.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the lack of CPU, memory, or free disk space on the Veeam ONE database server
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue make sure that the Veeam ONE server and Veeam ONE database server have a sufficient amount of CPU, memory, and free disk space and meet the system requirements. If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication server data collection delay
knowledgeSummary: The Veeam Backup & Replication server exceeded the maximum allowed data collection delay specified in the alarm settings threshold.
knowledgeCause: This may happen because of the following reasons: Veeam Backup & Replication server performance bottlenecks and connectivity issues Veeam Enterprise Manager server performance bottlenecks and connectivity issues Heavy load on the entire infrastructure
knowledgeResolution: A single alarm should not be considered as an issue. This behavior could be caused by many routine factors. To investigate the issue, perform the following actions: check Veeam Backup & Replication server and Veeam Enterprise Manager server general performance test WMI connectivity by following this KB article If the issue persists, collect Veeam ONE log files and contact Veeam Customer Support to open a support case. It is recommended that you provide the whole content of the log folders to ensure that all comprehensive information is provided to the support team for analysis.

name: Immutability change tracking (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: The configured immutability period has been changed on one or several Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 backup repositories
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 provides the capability to make the protected data immutable, thus resilient to cyber threats such as ransomware and malicious deletions. When the immutability period of these repositories changes, this alarm triggers.
knowledgeResolution: Make sure the immutability setting of the affected backup repositories is configured according to the internal security policy. To view the configuration settings: In Veeam ONE Client, open the Summary tab of the corresponding backup repository. In Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 Client, open the Backup Infrastructure tab, then navigate to the affected backup repository, and review the settings.

name: Backup proxy maintenance mode state (Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365)
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE has detected that a proxy has maintenance mode enabled. Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 Proxy maintenance mode is helpful when administrators need to perform some maintenance, upgrade, patching or similar activities. On the other hand, it can lead to unexpected job durations, missing RPO windows, and performance degradation, if the jobs were fine-tuned with consideration that all the threads on the other proxies are available.
knowledgeCause: A Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 administrator has enabled maintenance mode in proxy
knowledgeResolution: After the maintenance tasks are completed, or if the maintenance mode is not desired, go to the Veeam Backup for Microsoft 365 Console > Backup Infrastructure, right-click the necessary proxies, and disable the maintenance mode.

name: Cloud instance RPO
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE has detected that a cloud instance has missed its RPO (Recovery Point Objective). This can lead to potential data loss and be non-compliant within your SLA policies. This may occur due to resource bottlenecks, failed jobs, or misconfigured policies.
knowledgeCause: The cloud instance failed to meet the defined RPO due to resource constraints, job failures, or policy misconfigurations in the backup infrastructure.
knowledgeResolution: Review the Protected Cloud Instances report in Veeam ONE Web Client to identify the instances and jobs affected. Additionally, verify the cloud protection policies in Veeam ONE Monitor under Veeam Backup & Replication > Data Protection tab. Ensure all cloud proxies and resources are operating as expected, that the backup accounts have all the permissions to write on the backup repositories, and fine-tune the backup jobs or reallocate resources if necessary.

name: VM Backup RPO (oVirt KVM, Proxmox VE, Nutanix AHV)
knowledgeSummary: This VM has not been backed up within the defined RPO (Recovery Point Objective) or does not have any backup configured.
knowledgeCause: The Veeam backup job for this VM did not complete successfully, or there is no backup job configured for this VM.
knowledgeResolution: Verify the configuration of this VM in Veeam Backup & Replication. Check if this VM is part of an active backup job. If not, configure a new backup job for this VM. If the VM is part of an existing job, review the job's status and logs for any errors, and retry the job to create a restore point for this VM.

name: Veeam ONE Caching service state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE Caching service has failed.
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE Caching service is not started or not working properly. If this service is stopped, report caching will be unavailable, potentially leading to increased report generation time.
knowledgeResolution: Restart Veeam ONE Caching service. Type services.msc in the Start Search box, then press Enter. Right-click on the Veeam ONE Caching service and restart. Review Windows Event Log to find out the reason. Contact Veeam support if assistance is needed.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster created
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster has been created
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster was successfully created. The action could be performed by any user with sufficient permissions to configure HA clusters in the Veeam Backup & Replication.
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved, review your organization?s security procedures and check the audit events to identify the user who created the cluster.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster disassembled
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster has been disassembled
knowledgeCause: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster was successfully disassembled. The action could be performed by any user with sufficient permissions to configure HA clusters in the Veeam Backup & Replication.
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved, review your organization?s security procedures and check the audit events to identify the user who disassembled the cluster.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster primary node state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster primary node state has been changed to offline
knowledgeCause: This alarm is triggered when the Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster primary node is offline. This might be an approved administrator action or cluster node availability state change..
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved: Verify current state in the Veeam ONE Web UI - Configuration - Data Collection tab and check the status and review the logs of the Veeam Analytics service Verify cluster state in the Veeam Backup & Replication UI Check that all cluster nodes are reachable in the network

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster failover state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster failover has been initiated
knowledgeCause: The failover operation for the Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster has been initiated. The action could be performed by any user with sufficient permissions to configure HA clusters in the Veeam Backup & Replication.
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved, review your organization?s security procedures and check the audit events to identify the user who initiated the failover.

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster switchover state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster switchover has been initiated
knowledgeCause: The switchover operation for the Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster has been initiated. The action could be performed by any user with sufficient permissions to configure HA clusters in the Veeam Backup & Replication.
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved, review your organization?s security procedures and check the audit events to identify the user who initiated the switchover.

name: Recon Scanner threat state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam ONE received a security finding from Coveware Recon Scanner that matches an adversary technique mapped to MITRE ATT&CK. The finding indicates potentially malicious activity in the Veeam environment. Review the MITRE technique in the alarm details and open the Coveware portal for full context, timeline, and recommended actions.
knowledgeCause: Recon Scanner raised this alert after correlating one or more behaviors, for example: Repeated failed logons, unusual logon sources, or privilege escalation attempts Unexpected internal or external connections to Veeam components Suspicious file or process activity on protected hosts Installation or execution of unapproved tools or scripts Changes to Veeam settings, credentials, or repository access Indicators of data staging or exfiltration The alarm details list the host, user, timestamps, artifact, and the mapped MITRE technique ID.
knowledgeResolution: Validate and triage In the alarm details, note hostname, user, timestamps, artifact, risk level, and MITRE technique Open the Coveware portal to review the event timeline, related findings, and guidance Correlate with Veeam Backup & Replication session logs and Windows event logs for the same period Contain Isolate the affected host from the network Disable or reset the involved accounts and revoke active sessions Block the suspicious source IP or process in EDR or firewall Safeguard backups Verify immutability on repositories and object storage Pause risky operations such as file copy or tape export if advised by your IR process Rotate credentials used by backup infrastructure components Export the Veeam Backup & Replication configuration for recovery Verify clean recovery points Use SureBackup or Secure Restore to validate restore points in an isolated environment before recovery Restore only from validated clean points Post-incident hardening Enforce MFA for consoles and critical accounts Patch affected systems and remove unapproved tools Review RBAC assignments and restrict remote access where not required Keep Recon Scanner enabled and schedule regular scans

name: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster secondary node state
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster secondary node state has been changed to offline
knowledgeCause: This alarm is triggered when the Veeam Backup & Replication HA cluster secondary node is offline. This might be an approved administrator action or cluster node availability state change.
knowledgeResolution: If this was an approved action, no further steps are required. If this action was not expected or approved: Verify current state in the Veeam ONE Web UI - Configuration - Data Collection tab and check the status and review the logs of the Veeam Analytics service Verify cluster state in the Veeam Backup & Replication UI Check that all cluster nodes are reachable in the network

name: Backup Server Failed Logon Attempt
knowledgeSummary: This alarm triggers when authentication to the Veeam Backup Server or its services fails. Typical sources include the Veeam Backup & Replication console, Enterprise Manager, PowerShell or REST integrations, and Veeam components that connect with stored credentials. It helps you spot misconfiguration and unwanted access attempts.
knowledgeCause: Authentication usually fails due to an incorrect or expired password, account lockout/disablement, or wrong domain/UPN. It can also be triggered by stale stored credentials in third-party scripts using API or PowerShell using old credentials, or time drift causing Kerberos errors.
knowledgeResolution: As this alarm triggers on logon failures, check any scheduled script, or third-party integration that signs in to the Veeam Backup Server and update credentials that are incorrect, expired, or recently rotated. If no legitimate source is found, treat it as a potential brute-force or denial-of-service attempt: review authentication logs, restrict source IPs or interfaces, enforce account lockout policy, and rotate the affected secrets.

name: Backup Server Successful Logon
knowledgeSummary: This alarm triggers when a user or client completes a successful authorization against Veeam Backup & Replication. Typical sources include the Veeam Backup & Replication console, Enterprise Manager, REST or PowerShell clients, SAML2 or GSS logins, and other interactive flows. Use it to maintain a defensible audit trail of legitimate access and to spot unusual but successful logons that may indicate credential misuse.
knowledgeCause: A valid authentication and authorization flow completed. Common cases: Operator signed in via Veeam Backup & Replication Console, or Veeam Backup & Replication Web Console completed an interactive authorization code flow, SSO-backed sign-ins, including GSS or SAML2, completed as expected.
knowledgeResolution: No action is required for routine activity. Review each occurrence for hygiene and anomaly detection: Confirm actor and client: check FriendlyName, ClientId, and ResourceId, validate source IP: ensure SourceIpAddress is expected, check flow type: confirm the grant was an interactive method for this alarm?s intent. Harden if needed: if access is unexpected, tighten allowlists, rotate credentials, enforce MFA where applicable, and review adjacent failures around the same time window.

name: Potential Agent Management jobs failure
knowledgeSummary: In rare cases, ports utilized by Veeam Agent for Windows and Veeam Agent for Linux for communicating with Veeam Backup Server when operating in Agent Management mode can be used by a third-party process or by an existing instance of Veeam Backup Service if the service has been restarted recently. As a result, Agent backup jobs managed by Veeam Backup & Replication server may fail. The issue is observed in all Veeam Backup & Replication versions.
knowledgeCause: This could happen due to a variety of reasons including, but not limited to: Port is used by a third-party process; After Veeam Backup Service restart, the connection has not been closed yet, thus still using the port.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue makes sure there are no third-party applications utilizing the ports 1000N. Additionally, try to stop Veeam Backup Service, wait for about thirty seconds and start the service again, then make sure if Managed Agent jobs operate properly. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, please make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID when opening a support case. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID186166

name: Backup jobs failed to remove HPE Nimble storage snapshots
knowledgeSummary: Backup jobs utilizing HPE Nimble storage integration may not remove storage snapshots from SAN or even fail when using Veeam Backup & Replication 9.5 Update 3 or later.
knowledgeCause: This happens because of a missing delay before the snapshot removal command is sent to the storage by Nimble API.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private hotfix. When opening a support case, please make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID196382

name: RCE vulnerability in Veeam ONE agent components
knowledgeSummary: The currently installed versions of Veeam ONE agent components contain two critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerabilities which allow executing malicious code remotely without authentication. Veeam ONE versions affected by this issue are: 9.5 Update 4, Update 4a and version 10.
knowledgeCause: The issue happens because of improper deserealization of untrusted data.
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the issue and a solution, refer to the KB3144. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID when opening a support case. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID226293

name: License revoke finished with false success
knowledgeSummary: License revoke may silently fail and finish with false success in UI (operation is marked successfully in UI, but the previously licensed instances are not revoked). The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v11 RTM (build 11.0.0.825), v11 GA (build 11.0.0.837), v11a (build 11.0.1.1261), v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for versions 11, 11a and 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the stored procedure responsible for the licensed instances revoke in Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID477147

name: Expanding the "Tapes" tab leads to "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication console may show the "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error message when the user opens the Files view and tries to access the files copied to tape. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the stored procedure responsible for extracting tape server information from the Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230412 or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID487298

name: Kasten integration services didn't start due to certificate error
knowledgeSummary: Services responsible for integration with Kasten K10 may fail to start on Veeam Backup & Replication server with the "Certificate was not found" error message. The issue was confirmed on Veeam Backup & Replication machines with Dutch (Netherlands) and Luxembourgish (Luxembourg) system locales. This issue may affect also not Kasten-related activities (e.g. configuration backup). The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a short date format used in the above listed system locales, which makes the required certificate to be generated with an invalid date.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or set the short date format as d-m-yyyy as described on the official Microsoft website. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID487302

name: Potential issues with NetApp MetroCluster integration
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication users may face various error messages during NetApp MetroCluster rescan and failures during NetApp MetroCluster backup. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by invalid handling of the internal NetApp MetroCluster errors in the Veeam Backup & Replication component responsible for the storage system.
knowledgeResolution: To prevent the issue from occurring and resolve the observed issues with NetApp MetroCluster integration, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230412 or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. name: Security vulnerability CVE-2023-27532 in Veeam Backup & Replication
knowledgeSummary: Vulnerability CVE-2023-27532 allows to obtain encrypted credentials stored in the configuration database, which may lead to gaining access to the backup infrastructure hosts. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v11a (build 11.0.1.1261), v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20220302 (build 11.0.1.1261 P20220302) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for 11a.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by Veeam.Backup.Service.exe malfunction which allows an unauthenticated access to encrypted credentials.
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the issue and resolution, refer to the KB4424. The vulnerability is fixed in the Cumulative Patch P20230227 for the version 11a and Cumulative Patch P20230223 for the version 12. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID when opening a support case. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID487304

name: SQL server transaction log backup failed to complete with "Failed to install Log Shipping Service using connect to guest agent through VIX via GIP"
knowledgeSummary: SQL server transaction log backup may fail to complete with the "Failed to install Log Shipping Service using connect to guest agent through VIX via GIP" error message when the parent backup job is configured to process VMs and containers added from VMware Cloud Director. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the VM search algorithm which mistakenly uses VMware Cloud Director VM UUID instead of VMware MoRef ID (Managed Object Reference ID).
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230412 or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. name: Enabling immutability option on Amazon S3 external repository finished with false success
knowledgeSummary: Adding new Amazon S3 external repository with the Make backups immutable for the entire duration of their retention policy option selected in the repository settings may finish with false success in the Veeam Backup & Replication console: although the immutability option is selected, immutability remains disabled. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by invalid handling of the exception for lack of permissions on the account used to add the affected Amazon S3 object storage to Veeam Backup & Replication console.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230412 and add the required permissions to the account used to add the affected Amazon S3 object storage to Veeam Backup & Replication console. The required permissions are s3:GetBucketVersioning and s3:GetBucketObjectLockConfiguration. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. knowledgeSummary: Ejecting tape operation may fail to complete with the "Automated library not found" error message when ejecting a tape from a standalone drive. knowledgeSummary: File share backup job may fail to complete with the "Failed to perform applying backup retention for restore point" error message. knowledgeSummary: SOBR offload session may get stuck for an indefinite time with the "Updating backup chain index" message or fail to complete with the "Impossible to start a new log because there are other logs" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by incorrect handling of the situations when Veeam Backup & Replication triggers the previously created transaction logs deletion from the SOBR capacity tier extent, but the underlying object storage device for some reason preserves the files but reports successful deletion.

knowledgeSummary: SureBackup job may fail to complete with the "Proxy appliance VM network initialization failed" error message if multiple IPv4 virtual NICs are configured in the virtual lab selected in the affected job settings. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420) and Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the virtual lab starting procedure, which mistakenly assigns an extra non-existing IP address to the proxy appliance and fails to reach it afterwards.

name: Backup or replica job finished with "Failed to get guest OS path for newly attached disk" warning
knowledgeSummary: Backup or replica job may finish with the "Failed to get guest OS path for newly attached disk" warning or error message when using Linux-based backup proxies in the virtual appliance mode (HotAdd) for data processing. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by multipath daemon enabled on the affected Linux proxy servers; due to this functionality backup server may consider a disk attached to the proxy as duplicated if this disk if available from this proxy server via multipath.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or disable multipath daemon in the Linux operating system on the proxies selected to process data in the virtual appliance mode (HotAdd). If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. name: Backup to tape job failed to complete with "Invalid ACL data"
knowledgeSummary: Backup to tape job for NAS backup files may fail to complete with the "Invalid ACL data" error message or similar error messages also referring to ACL. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223) and Cumulative Patch P20230412 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230412).
knowledgeCause: The issue may be caused by several reasons affecting the source file share backups. Most commonly it is triggered by using special characters (e.g. , &, ' etc.) in the file names on the source file share. The special symbols might be translated and recorded incorrectly during source file share backup job which makes the backup files unreadable for the backup to tape job.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230718 or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by false positive corruption signal triggered during NAS backup retention policy execution. The library used for performing LZ4 compression got updated which lead to an incorrect comparison of the compressed chunks that have the same md5 hash.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230718 or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. name: Backup copy job creates excessive GFS restore points
knowledgeSummary: Backup copy job may start creating excessive GFS restore points on each job run if the Read the entire restore point from source instead of synthesizing it from increments option is enabled in the job settings. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the long-term retention policy retry algorithm which strikes if the backup copy job was earlier interrupted during GFS restore point creation in the active full method. The created GFS restore point is marked as incomplete and this flag fails to be cleaned even after successful job retry.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID550983

name: Backup job failed to complete with "Item is locked by running session Retention job"
knowledgeSummary: Backup job may fail to complete with the "Item is locked by running session Retention job" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the files lock release algorithm which causes freeze during background retention and leads to the subsequent backup job failure. This situation may occur in various scenarios, e.g. if the affected backup files were locked by a restore process when the background retention process started.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. name: Plug-in jobs to Dell EMC Data Domain and HPE StoreOnce performance may be significantly decreased
knowledgeSummary: Plug-in backup jobs (SAP HANA, Oracle RMAN, SAP on Oracle) utilizing target repository running on Dell EMC Data Domain or HPE StoreOnce storage appliance may experience significant performance degradation after upgrade to Veeam Backup & Replication version 12. knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by changes in the Veeam Data Mover component working on the repository gateway side which starts in one instance and forces asynchronous writing commands to the target repository. This workflow is suboptimal for writing data to Dell EMC Data Domain and HPE StoreOnce storage appliances.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. name: File to tape job silently skips files located on NTFS volumes with enabled deduplication from processing
knowledgeSummary: File to tape job may silently skip the files located on NTFS volumes with enabled deduplication from processing. name: Offload job failed to complete with "Storage does not exist. Failed to process method ArchRepo.CreateCheckpoint"
knowledgeSummary: Offload job may fail to complete with the "Storage does not exist. Failed to process method ArchRepo.CreateCheckpoint" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230223 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230223) and Cumulative Patch P20230412 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230412).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the rescan algorithm which strikes when the latest checkpoint (file containing meta information about the state of offloaded backup chains) is inconsistent for some reasons. When the rescan occurs, it uses data from the latest consistent checkpoint. That might lead to a collision in the Veeam Backup & Replication database.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. To prevent the issue from further occurring, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the Cumulative Patch P20230718. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID550993

knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication console may pop up the message "Could not create unique index backups_temp_id_idx" at startup and fail to display all elements on the Backups view. name: Backup job failed to complete with "Failed to create snapshot for LUN. The request could not be understood by the server. Unexpected argument"
knowledgeSummary: Backup job may fail to complete with the "Failed to create snapshot for LUN. The request could not be understood by the server. Unexpected argument" error message. The issue happens when Veeam Backup & Replication is instructed to use Backup from Storage Snapshots feature to process VMs in the affected job. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the algorithm of interaction with the Nimble volumes accessed over FC. It triggers when Veeam Backup & Replication sends a command to create a snapshot including the "multi_initiator" parameter, not supported by such volumes.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. knowledgeSummary: Backup copy job for HPE StoreOnce repositories may sporadically fail to complete with the "Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint 'PK_Storages'" error message when the job tries to copy synthetic full backup files located in the source repository. knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a collision in Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database which appears when data transfer between the source and target HPE StoreOnce repositories is interrupted due to network connectivity issues and the backup copy job has to be retried.

knowledgeSummary: Backup to tape job may fail to complete with the "Unable to allocate new synthesized storage metadata bank. Banks limit was reached" error message when processing backup files located in an object storage repository. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the component responsible for enumerating the backup files in the source object storage repository. Due to hardcoded software limitations, the component fails to process backup files with the number of unique blocks exceeding a certain limit.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. name: Dell EMC Data Domain repository rescan procedure performance may be significantly decreased
knowledgeSummary: Dell EMC Data Domain repository rescan procedure may experience significant performance degradation after upgrade to Veeam Backup & Replication version 12. knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by library update in the Veeam Data Mover component working on the repository gateway side. The update caused performance degradation due to increased time required to open every new connection to the Dell EMC Data Domain repository.

name: Jobs to Dell EMC Data Domain sporadically fail with "Nothing matched", "The user has insufficient privileges" or "Filesystem is read-only"
knowledgeSummary: Jobs targeted to a Dell EMC Data Domain repository may sporadically fail with the "Nothing matched. Err: 5004", "The user has insufficient privilege. Err: 5034" or "Filesystem is read-only. Err: 5044" error messages. The issue occurs if Veeam Backup & Replication installation has two or more Dell EMC Data Domain repositories added, and these repositories are physically located on the same storage device but utilize different shares. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the Veeam Data Mover component working on the repository gateway side. The malfunction leads to an unexpected connection interruption when two or more repositories are used simultaneously in the setup described in the Knowledge section.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. knowledgeSummary: NAS backup or backup copy job may fail to complete with the "Unable to transfer metadata: flat metadata generation detected" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by race condition appearing when the affected job target repository rescan and metadata file transformation occur at the same time, which leads to a collision in Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. knowledgeSummary: Removing backup from capacity tier may fail to complete with the "Not applicable. Use DehydrateStg instead" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the backup removal mechanism, which strikes when the affected backup chain is created in the single-file backup format and with the reverse incremental backup method. The issue may appear after one or more VMs are removed from processing in the source job, which changes the reverse incremental backup files status in the capacity tier.

knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication console may crash at the Guest Processing step of the Edit Job wizard. name: Restore to Amazon EC2 failed to complete with "Failed to validate machine: Cannot find a suitable image for the restored machine"
knowledgeSummary: Restore to Amazon EC2 may fail to complete with the "Failed to validate machine: Cannot find a suitable image for the restored machine" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v11 RTM (build 11.0.0.825), v11 GA (build 11.0.0.837), v11a (build 11.0.1.1261), v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for versions 11, 11a and 12.

knowledgeSummary: Restore from a configuration backup file created for a backup server running a Microsoft SQL Server database may fail with the ''There is an error in XML document'' error message if the target server runs a PostgreSQL database. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by improper XML data located in the session-related table of the original Veeam Backup & Replication configuration database.

name: File share backup job failed to complete with "Failed to process method {NasMaster.RemoveSourceBackupMeta}: Operation not permitted"
knowledgeSummary: File share backup job targeted to an immutable repository may fail to complete with the "Failed to process method {NasMaster.RemoveSourceBackupMeta}: boost::filesystem::remove: Operation not permitted" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in retention procedure that unlocks the job XML metadata files for removal but fails to remove immutability flag from those files. This, in its turn, leads to a collision when the Veeam Data Mover component tries to remove the files in accordance with retention policy, but removal fails due to the immutability flag on those files.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID608325

knowledgeSummary: Scale-out backup repository rescan may fail with the: "Unable to import backup: a backup already exists in the configuration database" error message when rescanning backups located in capacity tier. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.

knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1 or contact Veeam Technical support to get a private fix. knowledgeSummary: "Object reference not set to an instance of an object" error message pops up in Veeam Backup & Replication console every time "Re-Check" button is pressed in Missing Updates window. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718).
