knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to the version 12.1. Note that this error does not impact Veeam Backup & Replication functionality and can be ignored. name: Entire VM restore from Enterprise Manager ignores the new VM name
knowledgeSummary: When the Entire VM Restore of a VMware Cloud Director VM to a different location is initiated in Enterprise Manager, the new VM name value is ignored. As the VM is restored with the original name, the machine in the production site can be overwritten. Parameters tab in Restoring VM window show the 'VM name' being equal to the original VM name and not the one specified during the restore attempt. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by Enterprise Manager ignoring the new VM name in the Destination tab and using the original one.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) or contact Veeam Technical support for a private fix. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure you provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. name: SureBackup job failed to start with "Cannot start SureBackup job to the latest restore point as some linked jobs are still running"
knowledgeSummary: SureBackup job fails to start by schedule or manually with the "Cannot start SureBackup job to the latest restore point as some linked jobs are still running. You can use Start To option to select a previous restore point, or try again later" error message. This happens when there is an SQL log backup job or another continuous linked job running. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a malfunction in the job dependencies search algorithm in Veeam Backup Service.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) or contact Veeam Technical support for a private fix. name: Veeam Guest Catalog Service 100% CPU usage due to malware detection issue
knowledgeSummary: Malware detection activity performed by Veeam Guest Catalog Service may result in 100% CPU usage when the queue is full and VM processing takes more than 2 minutes. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131).
knowledgeCause: In case when the malware detection queue is full and all VMs are being scanned for more than 2 minutes, VMs waiting for the processing are removed from the queue and remain in unhandled state until the service is restarted. This can result in a loop of tasks.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) or contact Veeam Technical support for a private fix. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure you provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID626193

name: Potential issues with file to tape jobs copying nothing after upgrade to v12.1
knowledgeSummary: File to tape job may back up no files and be marked as successful in both UI and job report. In Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1, "include everything" filter inclusion mask is not set as a default value for jobs configured before the upgrade to v12.1 with no file mask set. Note that this issue occurs when the file to tape job is configured to process data from Windows and/or SMB shares only. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by source folder filter mask logic not being updated on previously configured file to tape jobs to "include everything" default value when upgrading to Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) or, in the Files and Folders step of the File to Tape Job wizard, use Files by mask option to add \* as the inclusion filter in the Include masks field for all affected data sources. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID632054

name: SureBackup job from a Veeam Agent for Windows backup fails with "System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException" after v12.1 update
knowledgeSummary: A SureBackup job in a Hyper-V environment for a machine backed up with Veeam Agent for Windows may fail with an "Actual value was Unknown (System.ArgumentOutOfRangeException)" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131).
knowledgeCause: When collecting the busType parameter from the backup metadata, the wrong field is used making the parameter unknown for the SureBackup job.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) or contact Veeam Technical support for a private fix. knowledgeSummary: Cisco HyperFlex backup jobs and storage rescans fail with the "Object reference not set to an instance of an object. knowledgeSummary: Backup copy job processing backup files created by the file backup job may fail to complete with the "Item [restore point] is locked by running session" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131) and 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56).

name: Deleting an expired immutability blob fails with "This operation is not permitted as the blob is immutable due to a policy." on Azure Data Box object storage repository
knowledgeSummary: Trying to delete an old version of the blob with expired immutability on the Azure Data Box object storage repository may fail with "This operation is not permitted as the blob is immutable due to a policy." error message. knowledgeCause: The problem is caused by Azure-specific behavior that requires a delete request to be made without a version in order to delete the current version of the blob. High volume of 'Unique identifier is invalid.' exceptions may cause performance issues and scheduling delays. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131), 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) and 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172).

name: False positive notifications of potential malware activity due to onion link detection
knowledgeSummary: A false positive notification of potential malware activity may occur due to onion link detection after the update of Veeam Backup & Replication to the latest version. The pagefile.sys file may also appear in a backup even if the 'Exclude swap file blocks' option is selected as another symptom of the same issue. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172).
knowledgeCause: The error occurs as a result of swap file exclusion operation. Certain disk blocks with parts of the pagefile.sys file are copied to the target even if the 'Exclude swap file blocks' option is selected. Anti-virus signatures originating from the pagefile.sys file may remain in the backup due to this behavior and are interpreted as potential malware activity.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334) or if, in addition to getting notifications of potential malware activity due to onion link detection, the pagefile.sys file appears in the backup with the 'Exclude swap file blocks' option selected, please contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. name: Backup to tape job fails with "Cannot find recovery record for backup with ID / Failed to recrypt crypto keys for tape backup Id" error
knowledgeSummary: Backup to tape job may fail with "Cannot find recovery record for backup with ID / Failed to recrypt crypto keys for tape backup Id" error if both encrypted and non-encrypted chains are present in the source agent job and the latest chain is not encrypted. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) and 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172).
knowledgeCause: During the tape job session, checks are performed to determine the current encryption state of the backup and if the latest full storage doesn't have storage keys, the logic incorrectly assumes that the backup is not encrypted causing a failure for subsequent actions.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve this issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. name: Direct backup to object storage fails with "The given key was not present in the dictionary"
knowledgeSummary: Background retention can take a long time when using direct-to-object storage with a large backup job. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56) and 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172).

name: NetApp NFS share backup fails with "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v3 to NFS v4" after upgrade to 12.3.1.1139
knowledgeSummary: After upgrading to VBR 12.3.1.1139, some NFS shares on the NetApp filer may fail to backup with the error "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v3 to NFS v4. Please revert protocol changes or start a new backup chain". The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) and v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165).
knowledgeCause: The problem occurs when prior versions of VBR cannot detect the version of the NFS protocol of these shares. This causes VBR to use NFS 3 as the default protocol. After the upgrade, the NFS 4.1 protocol starts working and NAS backup tries to connect to the NFS share with the highest available protocol.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. name: Nutanix filer backup job fails with "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v4 to NFS v3."
knowledgeSummary: After upgrading to VBR 12.3.0.310, an NFS share on a Nutanix filer backup may fail with the error "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v4 to NFS v3. Please revert protocol changes or start a new backup chain". The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3 (build 12.3.0.310).
knowledgeCause: A faulty NFS protocol version check resulted in the forced usage of a lower protocol version than required, causing the error.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. name: Rescan of the CDP replicas fails with "Sequence contains more than one matching element"
knowledgeSummary: The "Sequence contains more than one matching element" error may occur while rescanning and attempting to remove specific replicas from the UI console. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.2 (build 12.2.0.334).
knowledgeCause: Information about the replicas were not cleared leading to synchronization failure and the "Sequence contains more than one matching element" error.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. name: Security vulnerability CVE-2025-48983 in Veeam Backup & Replication
knowledgeSummary: The vulnerability CVE-2025-48983 allows for remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup infrastructure hosts and only impacts domain-joined Veeam Backup & Replication v12 backup infrastructure servers. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication version 12.3.2.3617 and all earlier version 12 builds.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a vulnerability in the Mount service of Veeam Backup & Replication, which allows for remote code execution (RCE) on the Backup infrastructure hosts by an authenticated domain user.
knowledgeResolution: To get more information about the issue and resolution, refer to the following Veeam Support Knowledge Base article KB4771. The vulnerability is resolved in the Veeam Backup & Replication 12.3.2 Patch (12.3.2.4165). name: "Parent encrypted backup not found. Please check DB" message pops up when navigating the Backups node
knowledgeSummary: Veeam Backup & Replication console may pop up the "Parent encrypted backup not found. Please check DB" when navigating the Backups view if it contains encrypted backups imported from an object storage repository. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v11a (build 11.0.1.1261), v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131), V12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56), 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172), 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334), 12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), 12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4465), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 11a and version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a collision in the encryption status which usually strikes when the affected backup chain has an empty metadata file (e.g. if the backup chain format was switched from single-file backup to per-machine backup previously).
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, decrypt the affected imported encrypted backups with the PowerShell commandlet. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID532304

name: Scale-out backup repository rescan procedure failed to complete with "Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint"
knowledgeSummary: Scale-out backup repository rescan procedure may fail to complete with the "Violation of PRIMARY KEY constraint 'PK_Backup.Model.Points_id'. Cannot insert duplicate key in object 'dbo.Backup.Model.Points'. The duplicate key value is ([GUID])" error message when trying to import previously uploaded backup chains from an object storage repository. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12 RTM (build 12.0.0.1402), v12 GA (build 12.0.0.1420), v12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131), 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56), 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172), 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334), 12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), 12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4465), Cumulative Patch P20230718 (build 12.0.0.1420 P20230718) and all the previous Cumulative Patches released for the version 12.
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by a discrepancy between the affected backup chain metadata files and the actual backup chain which strikes if this backup chain was created in the previous Veeam Backup & Replication version in the legacy per-machine backup with single metadata file format.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID608330

name: Configuration backup failed to complete with "Configuration database is corrupted"
knowledgeSummary: Configuration backup performed on a Veeam Backup & Replication server configured for a service provider role may fail to complete with the "Configuration database is corrupted" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1 (build 12.1.0.2131), 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56), 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172), 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334), 12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), 12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165) and 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4465).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by corrupted records in the Tenants. The tokens table may have been corrupted during preceding configuration database migration.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, contact Veeam Technical support for a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure you provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID660973

name: NAS backup job fails with "Failed to process method {NasMaster.SaveSourceBackupMeta}: File already exist"
knowledgeSummary: NAS jobs with an SMB repository as a primary target may fail on updating vsource or vsourcecopy metadata due to known issues with SMB cache. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.1.1 (build 12.1.1.56), 12.1.2 (build 12.1.2.172), 12.2 (build 12.2.0.334), 12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), 12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) and 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by metadata being updated too frequently. The bug was mitigated in previous VBR versions but the changes were rolled back due to a separate bug.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support. When opening a support case, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID843756

name: Log backup is performed twice during the same run
knowledgeSummary: When the log backup interval is set to 60 minutes, log backup may be performed twice in the same run, resulting in "Log chain for database [DATABASE_NAME] cannot be applied to a full backup" error. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.2 (build 12.2.0.334), v12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), v12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) and v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165)
knowledgeCause: Root cause is related to RPC connectivity breaking during calls to backup logs resulting in a duplication of calls.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. name: NAS backup job failed to complete with "Container doesn't exist in backup"
knowledgeSummary: The file share backup job may fail to complete with the "Container doesn't exist in backup" error message. This happens when the Use native changed files tracking checkbox is enabled in the properties of the NAS filer used as a source in the affected job. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), v12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) and 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165) and affects NetApp storage systems added as NAS filers.
knowledgeCause: The issue occurs when NetApp native changed files tracking data contains info about the objects not added to file share backup, hidden system directories or excluded folders.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. name: NAS backup job failed to complete with "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v3 to NFS v4"
knowledgeSummary: The file share backup job may fail to complete with the "Unable to process the file share: source was changed from NFS v3 to NFS v4" error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3 (build 12.3.0.310), v12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139), 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) and 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165) and affects NetApp storage systems added as NAS filers.
knowledgeCause: A preceding hidden issue causes this problem during rescanning of NFS access validation to the shares on the affected NetApp storage system. Later, when validation is successful, NFS 4.1 use is forced, which can cause a conflict if NFS 3 was already used for this NAS filer.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID967156

name: Potential issues with Veeam Backup & Replication log folder size
knowledgeSummary: Moving backup data to the archive tier process might generate excessive logging and cause the Veeam log folder on the Veeam Backup & Replication server to overflow. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3.1 (build 12.3.1.1139).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by archival sessions endlessly looping at writing the backup files which were detected to freeze and move to the archive tier. As a result, archival session log excessively grows which might cause Veeam Backup & Replication server to run out of free space on a disk that stores Veeam log folder.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID967157

name: Unhealthy VBR status related to the WMI ObjectInJob class collection issue
knowledgeSummary: After upgrading Veeam ONE to version 13.0.0.5630, 13.0.1.5860, 13.0.1.5924 or 13.0.1.6168, data collection from backup servers in the monitoring system consistently shows the 'Unhealthy' status. The problem is persistent and affects all available backup servers, with data collection sessions exceeding the maximum allowed timeout. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.3617), v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4165) and v12.3.2 (build 12.3.2.4465).
knowledgeCause: Veeam ONE is waiting for the failed ObjectInJob class collection from VBR resulting in an exceeded data collection timeout.
knowledgeResolution: To solve the issue with the ObjectInJob class collection, recreate an EntraID job. VBR status should be Healthy again in Web Client afterwards. For more details, refer to KB0043868: WMI ObjectInJob class cannot be collected, causing issues with VBR monitoring by Veeam ONE. If you are about to contact Veeam Technical Support regarding this alarm, make sure to provide Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID1140956

knowledgeCause: Destination IndexData folder is removed by the GuestHelper process before data transfer initiates.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to version 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. knowledgeSummary: If the backup job fails, manually attempting a retry fails with the "Unable to repair a backup file because it is immutable, please perform an active full backup instead" message. name: Restore to Azure Disk fails with 'Invalid Azure access tier string received'
knowledgeSummary: When attempting to restore Windows servers to Azure from backup, the process fails during the disk conversion step. The error message indicates an invalid Azure access tier string and a failure to mount the VHD disk, resulting in the restored VM potentially being unbootable. This issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 12.3.2.4165 and all earlier version 12 builds and also in Veeam Backup & Replication 13.0.1.1071 and all earlier version 13 builds.
knowledgeCause: Azure returns a disk SKU instead of an Access Tier type due to unawareness of specific Super Premium disks types that were improperly handled.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to Veeam Backup & Replication 12.3.2.4465 Patch or 13.0.1 P2 (13.0.1.2067), depending on your current version, or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID1318247

name: Editing jobs fails with 'Error while Resolving SID. Error number 1332' after configuration backup restore
knowledgeSummary: Editing backup and backup copy jobs after restoring the configuration database on a newly deployed VBR returns an "Error while resolving sid. Error number 1332." error message. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 13.0.1 (13.0.1.180) and 13.0.1 P1 (13.0.1.1071).
knowledgeCause: The issue is caused by the user which created this specific job not being present on the current server.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to 13.0.1 P2 (13.0.1.2067) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a private fix. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID1318249

name: Configuration Backup fails with 'Unsupported PostgreSQL type: character varying(255)'
knowledgeSummary: Configuration Backup fails with 'Unsupported PostgreSQL type: character varying(255)' when the PostgreSQL-based Veeam configuration database contains row(s) in the 'backup.model.trustedaddomainauthoritycertificates' table. These rows are typically created after using an AD Protection Group with LDAPS (port 636) and accepting/trusting the domain root CA certificate prompt. The issue occurs in Veeam Backup & Replication 13.0.1.1071 and all earlier version 13 builds.
knowledgeCause: The configuration backup logic does not handle the varchar(255) column type used in the 'backup.model.trustedaddomainauthoritycertificates' table.
knowledgeResolution: To resolve the issue, upgrade Veeam Backup & Replication to 13.0.1 P2 (13.0.1.2067) or contact Veeam Technical Support to get a workaround. When opening a support case, make sure to provide the Veeam Intelligent Diagnostics signature UID. This will simplify the investigation process and can significantly decrease resolution time. Signature UID: VID1318254
